What is UP Police Constable City Intimation Slip 2026?

The UP Police Constable City Intimation Slip 2026 is an advance notification document released by the Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB) before the admit card. It provides candidates with essential preliminary information about their examination to help them make necessary travel and accommodation arrangements. The city slip is NOT your admit card but serves as an important reference document.

Key Information Available in City Slip:

  • Allotted exam city and state
  • Scheduled exam date
  • Shift timing (Morning/Afternoon/Evening)
  • Reporting time at exam center
  • Gate closing time
  • Candidate's name and registration number
  • Category (SC/ST/OBC/General/EWS)

Important Notice: The city intimation slip is NOT your admit card and does not permit you to appear in the exam. It is only for informational purposes. You must download the admit card separately (available 4 days before exam) to enter the examination hall.

How to Download UP Police Constable City Intimation Slip 2026? (Step-by-Step Guide)

Step 1: Visit Official Website

  • Open your web browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Edge recommended)
  • Visit UPPRPB official website: uppbpb.gov.in
  • Ensure you have a stable internet connection
  • On the homepage, scroll down to "Latest Updates" or "Important Links" section
  • Look for the link: "UP Police Constable City Intimation Slip 2026" or "Download City Slip for Constable Recruitment 2026"
  • Click on the city intimation slip download link
  • You may be redirected to a candidate login page
  • Enter your Registration Number or Application Number
  • Enter your Date of Birth in DD/MM/YYYY format OR Password
  • Type the Captcha Code as shown in the image (case-sensitive)
  • Double-check all entered information for accuracy
  • Click on the "Submit" or "Login" button
  • Your city intimation slip will appear on the screen
  • Carefully verify all details displayed
  • Check: Name, Registration No., Exam City, Date, Shift Timing
  • Click on "Download" or "Save as PDF" button
  • Save the file to your computer/mobile device
  • Take a printout of the city slip for reference
  • Save a backup copy in cloud storage (Google Drive/Email)
  • Keep both digital and physical copies safe

Download your city intimation slip as soon as it is released (expected May 29-31, 2026) to avoid server overload and last-minute technical issues. The website may experience heavy traffic during peak hours.

Details Available in UP Police Constable City Intimation Slip 2026

When you download your city intimation slip, you will find the following information:

Information Type Details
Candidate Name Full name as per application form
Registration Number Unique application/registration ID
Exam Name UP Police Constable Examination 2026
Exam City/State Name of city and state where exam center is located
Exam Date Scheduled date of your examination (June 8, 9 or 10, 2026)
Shift Timing Morning Shift / Afternoon Shift / Evening Shift
Reporting Time Time to reach the exam center
Gate Closing Time Last entry time at exam center gate
Exam Duration 2 Hours
Category SC/ST/OBC/General/EWS
Important Instructions Dos and Don'ts for exam day

Note: The exact exam center address will be mentioned only in the admit card, which will be released 4 days before your scheduled exam date. The city slip only mentions the exam city name.

Key Highlights

FOLLOW US
  • Vacancy: DRDO RAC is recruiting 33 Scientists (Scientist E, D & C) under the DRDO/DRDS cadre through the Lateral Recruitment Scheme.
  • Qualification: First Division B.E./B.Tech, M.Sc., M.Pharmacy, MBBS/MD, or equivalent in relevant disciplines with 3–11 years of experience depending on the post.
  • Last Date: Online applications can be submitted until 19 June 2026 (4:00 PM).
  • Salary: Basic Pay ranges from ₹67,700 (Scientist C) to ₹1,23,100 (Scientist E) under the 7th CPC Pay Matrix.
  • Selection: Administrative Screening → Technical Screening → Personal Interview. Candidates must secure 75% marks in the final interview for selection.
  • Job Type: Regular Government Job under DRDO/DRDS cadre.
  • Apply Mode: Online only through the RAC recruitment portal.

Defence Research and Development Organisation Recruitment & Assessment Centre (DRDO RAC) has officially released the Scientist Recruitment 2026 notification (Advt. No. 157) for multiple vacancies across Scientist E, Scientist D, and Scientist C positions under the Defence Research & Development Service (DRDS) cadre. Eligible engineering and science professionals meeting the prescribed educational qualifications and experience requirements can apply online from 30 May 2026 to 19 June 2026 through the RAC recruitment portal. Selected candidates will be appointed on a regular permanent basis under the Lateral Recruitment Scheme with basic pay ranging from ₹67,700 to ₹1,23,100 per month under Pay Levels 11, 12, and 13, along with applicable allowances.

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC), Lucknow Road, Timarpur, Delhi – 110054 has officially released Advertisement No. 157 for Direct Recruitment of Scientists in DRDO cadre (Scientist E, Scientist D, Scientist C) and Encadered posts in DRDS cadre under Lateral Recruitment Scheme. The notification includes complete details on subject/discipline-wise vacancies, essential and desirable qualifications, experience requirements, age limit, application fee, selection process, and general instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying.

  • Download DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 Overview

DRDO RAC is recruiting Scientists (Scientist E, D, and C) across multiple engineering and science disciplines on regular basis under Lateral Recruitment Scheme, closing date 19 June 2026.

Organization Name Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), Recruitment & Assessment Centre (RAC), Lucknow Road, Timarpur, Delhi – 110054
Advertisement No. Advt. No. 157
Post Names Scientist E (Basic Pay: Rs. 1,23,100/-, Level 13), Scientist D (Basic Pay: Rs. 78,800/-, Level 12), Scientist C (Basic Pay: Rs. 67,700/-, Level 11)
Recruitment Type Direct Recruitment on Regular basis under Lateral Recruitment Scheme
Cadre DRDS (Defence Research & Development Service) cadre and Encadered posts
Application Mode Online only via https://rac.gov.in
Selection Mode Administrative Screening → Technical Screening (Level-1 & Level-2 if required) → Final Personal Interview (in-person at RAC, Delhi)
Minimum Score for Final Selection 75% marks (75 out of 100) in Final Personal Interview
Official Website https://rac.gov.in
Career Progression Excellent opportunities under Flexible Complimenting Scheme (FCS); merit-based promotion without vacancy linkage

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Advertisement Published in Employment News 30 May – 5 June 2026
Online Application Opens Available on https://rac.gov.in
Closing Date for Online Application 19 June 2026 (1600 hrs.)
Eligibility Cut-off Date 19 June 2026 (Closing Date of Advertisement)
Preliminary Online Interview / Final Personal Interview To be intimated through RAC website and Email/SMS

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

Vacancies are announced under two sections: (1) Regular posts in DRDS cadre and (2) Encadered posts in DRDS cadre. All above-mentioned vacancies are Unreserved (UR). (@) marked posts allow candidates from specific Divyang/PwD categories (Acid Attack Victims and Dwarfism only). (*) marked posts allow candidates from all Divyang/PwD categories except Blindness, low vision, Autism, Intellectual disability, Specific Learning disability and Mental Illness.

Section 1: Vacancies for Regular Posts in DRDS Cadre

Scientist 'E' – Basic Pay Rs. 1,23,100/- (Level 13 as per 7th CPC) – 01 Vacancy

Item No. No. of Vacancies Subject/Discipline Essential Qualifications & Experience Desirable
1 01 Electronics & Communication Engineering i. At least First Division Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or Technology in Electronics & Communication Engineering from a recognized university or equivalent.
ii. Minimum 11 years' of experience in Research or Design or Development in the field of Digital communication and engineering.		 i. Knowledge in ground and space-based communication systems and navigation.
ii. Knowledge in RTL Design and verification.
iii. Knowledge in handling test and measurement equipments like signal Generators, spectrum analysis and Network analysis.
iv. Post Doctoral / Doctorate Degree in Engineering or Master's Degree in Engineering or Technology in relevant field.
v. Knowledge of German, French, Russian, Japanese or Chinese.
vi. Three years' experience in Research and Development Management to manage and coordinate science & technology related activities in the area of Project Management or Material Management or Resource Management or Organisational Development.

Scientist 'D' – Basic Pay Rs. 78,800/- (Level 12 as per 7th CPC) – 07 Vacancies

Item No. No. of Vacancies Subject/Discipline Essential Qualifications & Experience (Summary)
2 01 Computer Science & Engineering* First Division BE/B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering + Minimum 7 years' experience in Research or Design or Development in Software Engineering. Desirable: Knowledge in C, C++, MATLAB/Simulink; simulation for space; Post Doctoral/Doctorate/Master's in relevant field; foreign language knowledge.
3 01 Aeronautical / Aerospace Engineering@ First Division BE/B.Tech in Aeronautical / Aerospace Engineering + Minimum 7 years' experience in Aero Configuration Design & Analysis and Aero Characterization. Desirable: Post Doctoral/Doctorate/Master's; German/French/Russian/Japanese/Chinese.
4 01 Mechanical Engineering@ First Division BE/B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering + Minimum 7 years' experience in research/design/development in: Design & analysis of mechanical systems of Gas turbine engine using Finite Element techniques, validation and testing. Desirable: Experience in turbo machinery/structural/thermal/hydraulic/modal/shock/fatigue/material non-linear/composite structure analysis; noise, vibration and shock mitigation strategies; Post Doctoral/Doctorate/Master's; foreign language.

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Only Indian Nationals can apply. The date for determining the eligibility of all candidates in every respect shall be the prescribed Closing Date (19 June 2026). First Division means 60% marks; where CGPA/CPI of 6.75 on a 10-point scale will be taken as equivalent to 60% as per AICTE guidelines. No rounding off of marks is permitted.

Criteria Details
Essential Academic Qualification At least First Division Bachelor's Degree in Engineering/Technology in the relevant discipline OR First Division Master's Degree in the relevant discipline from a recognized University. (As detailed post-wise above)
Experience Post-qualification experience only (experience counted only after passing the essential qualification). Scientist E: 11 years; Scientist D: 7 years; Scientist C: 3 years (as on closing date). Experience below Pay Level 10 as per 7th CPC or equivalent will not be considered.
CCA Declaration / NOC All serving candidates in Government/PSU/Government-promoted companies (with 50% or more controlling stake) must upload a signed CCA Declaration at time of online submission. NOC from Cadre Controlling Authority (CCA) is mandatory at time of Final Personal Interview.
Weightage for Higher Qualification Post Graduate Qualification in Engineering: 2 years; PG Degree in Medicine/Physiology (Medical Post only): 3 years; Doctorate Degree in Science: 3 years; PhD in Pharmacy: 3 years; PhD in Engineering: 4 years. (Only if in the relevant discipline and acquired before closing date; period of study must not overlap with work experience.)
Pay Equivalence (Private Sector) Candidates from Private Sector must submit proof of Pay drawn/Cost to Company (CTC)/CTC revision document/Form 16A during the period claimed as experience. Pay drawn/CTC/Form 16A will be the sole criteria. For details visit RAC website and check 'Pay Equivalence Criteria'.
Nationality Indian Nationals only

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age limit is as on the closing date of advertisement (19 June 2026). Maximum age shall not exceed 56 years including any relaxation. For Scientist C (Encadered posts) – there is no age relaxation under reserved categories.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
Scientist D / E Not exceeding 45 years
Scientist C Not exceeding 35 years
Divyang / PwD candidates Relaxable up to 10 years
Civilian Central Govt. Employees (with at least 3 years' continuous service on regular basis in same line or allied cadres) Relaxable up to 5 years
Ex-Servicemen (including Ex SSCOs/ECOs) Relaxable as per rules in vogue
Maximum age ceiling 56 years (including all relaxations)

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee is non-refundable and non-transferable, payable online only.

Category Application Fee
General / OBC / EWS male candidates Rs. 100/- (Rs. One Hundred only)
SC / ST / Divyang / Women candidates NIL (No application fee)

DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Screening of received applications will be carried out at multiple stages. Final selection is purely based on merit of marks scored in the Final Personal Interview.

Stage 1 – Administrative Screening: Verification of identity, age, essential & higher qualifications, length of suitable experience and CCA Declaration (wherever applicable).

Stage 2 – Technical Screening (Level-1): For all Administratively Eligible candidates - Technical Screening by holding a Meeting of Technical Screening Committee (an independent body including Technical Experts from Industry/Academia/DRDO other than RAC) using Bio-data based Technical Screening at Level-1 by: (a) Relevance of suitable experience w.r.t. advertised qualifications; (b) Relevance of Desirable/Higher Qualification; (c) Any other criteria suggested by Technical Screening Committee. Predefined Ratio for Preliminary Online Interview: Up to 24 candidates for 1 post; up to 48 candidates for 2 posts.

Stage 3 – Technical Screening (Level-2 / Preliminary Online Interview, if required): If number shortlisted for Final Personal Interview is large, further shortlisting via Preliminary Online Interview of short duration (10–15 minutes). Predefined Ratio for Final Personal Interview: Up to 12 candidates for 1 post; up to 24 candidates for 2 posts.

Stage 4 – Final Personal Interview (in-person at RAC, Delhi): Final selection is purely on merit of marks scored. Candidates must score a minimum of 75% marks (75 out of 100) in the Final Personal Interview for consideration for selection. The Final Personal Interview will be held in-person only before the Selection Board at RAC, Delhi on the scheduled date - no online/virtual interview will be considered.

Note: If administrative screening does not result in minimum 6 eligible candidates against one vacancy, the subsequent selection process for that vacancy may be cancelled. If technical screening does not result in minimum 6 candidates per vacancy for Final Personal Interview, the number of vacancies will be reduced accordingly.

Competition Level Analysis for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026

The competition level for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high, particularly for Scientist C posts in Electronics & Communication Engineering, Computer Science & Engineering, and Mechanical Engineering - disciplines with a large pool of eligible candidates and growing interest in DRDO's prestigious research environment. DRDO provides one of the best career growth structures for scientists under the Flexible Complimenting Scheme (FCS), making it a highly sought-after employer.

The multi-stage shortlisting process (Administrative → Technical Level-1 → Level-2/Preliminary Online Interview → Final Personal Interview in Delhi) is rigorous. Only up to 12 candidates per vacancy reach the Final Personal Interview, and even among those, a minimum of 75% marks in the interview is mandatory for selection. For Scientist D and E posts requiring 7–11 years of experience, the competition narrows to experienced professionals, but the specificity of required experience (e.g., Optics/Photonics, Radiation Physics, Chemical Engineering for underwater applications) naturally limits the eligible pool, making those posts relatively less competitive. Niche posts like Oceanography, Pharmacology, and Remote Sensing will see very limited candidates, improving selection chances for well-qualified applicants.

Who should NOT apply for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates who do not have First Division (60%+) in the essential qualifying degree - this is a hard minimum requirement and no rounding off is permitted.
  • Candidates with experience below Pay Level 10 (7th CPC) or equivalent - such experience will not be counted for computing the required years of experience.
  • Candidates whose period of study (higher qualification like PhD) overlaps with the work experience claimed - overlapping periods will be removed from total experience.
  • Candidates applying for Scientist D or E posts who are currently unemployed for more than one year - under current employment, the period of un-employment shall not exceed one year, else the candidature will be rejected.
  • Candidates serving in Government/PSU who do not upload a signed CCA Declaration - without CCA Declaration their candidature will be rejected, and NOC will be mandatory at Final Personal Interview.
  • Candidates from Private Sector who cannot produce CTC/Pay drawn proof/Form 16A for the claimed experience period - this is the sole criteria for establishing experience suitability from private sector.
  • Candidates exceeding the maximum age of 35 years (Scientist C), 45 years (Scientist D/E), or 56 years inclusive of all relaxations.
  • Candidates who cannot attend the Final Personal Interview in-person at RAC, Delhi - no online/virtual mode is available for Final Personal Interview under any circumstances.
  • Candidates who do not meet the specific work experience fields mentioned in the advertised qualifications - the Technical Screening Committee assesses relevance of experience very strictly.
  • Candidates applying for Scientist C (Encadered) posts from Reserved categories seeking age relaxation - there is no category-based age relaxation for Scientist C Encadered posts.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026?

  • Not checking whether your experience is at or above Pay Level 10 (7th CPC) equivalent - experience below this level is invalid and will not be counted.
  • Miscounting experience years by including the period of higher qualification (PhD/M.Tech) that overlaps with work experience - the overlapping period will be deducted.
  • Applying for multiple posts by submitting one application - candidates applying for more than one post must apply separately for each post and mention item numbers of all other posts where they have applied, along with the order of preference.
  • Not highlighting desirable qualifications and experience in the Justification column under 'Other Section' in the online application form - this is crucial for Technical Screening Committee's assessment.
  • Providing incorrect or non-verifiable experience details - the length of experience (date of joining along with Pay Scale/CTC and end date of each employment) should be available in attached experience documents.
  • Not getting experience certificates issued by Admin/HR Head/Director/Principal/Dean/Registrar/Competent Authority - certificates issued by Group Head/Division Head/Project Head/Professor or Head of Department are not acceptable.
  • Not converting CGPA to percentage before applying - CGPA/CPI of 6.75 on a 10-point scale equals 60%; candidates must provide conversion formula certificate from the university.
  • Changing registered mobile number or email after application - vital information will be communicated only to the registered contact; applicants are advised not to change these.
  • Locking/submitting the application without previewing it along with uploaded documents - no correction in data/application will be allowed and no document will be accepted once the application is finally submitted/locked.
  • Uploading documents in language other than English or Hindi without a notarized/certified transcript - all certificates in other languages must be accompanied by notarized transcripts.

How to Apply for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online through the RAC website (https://rac.gov.in) for filling the online application form. Applications received through any other mode will not be entertained. On successful registration, candidates may login and submit the online application before the closing date to avoid last minute network congestion. Only locked/finalised applications in all respects shall be considered. No correction in the data/application will be allowed and no document will be accepted once the application is finally submitted/locked.

Candidates should retain a printout/copy of the online application (.pdf format) after submission.

Important: ESSENTIAL ELIGIBILITY DOCUMENTS INCLUDING PROOFS FOR DATE OF BIRTH / ESSENTIAL QUALIFICATION / CCA DECLARATION (IF APPLICABLE) WILL NOT BE ACCEPTED AT ANY STAGE AFTER ADVERTISEMENT CLOSING DATE.

Documents Required at time of Interview (Originals + Copies):

  • Date of Birth (DOB) proof - Self attested Matriculation / High School certificate / Birth Certificate
  • A recent passport size colour photograph (not exceeding 30 KB)
  • Scanned sample of candidate's signature (not exceeding 30 KB)
  • Self-attested degree certificates/testimonials regarding Essential and Higher qualification along with mark-sheets
  • Fellowship proofs mentioning start date and end date of fellowship (if applicable)
  • Duly signed CCA Declaration (if applicable)
  • Notarized affidavit in support (if name mismatch in documents)
  • Notarized/certified transcript if documents are in language other than English or Hindi
  • All experience certificates (with start and end date) and salary Proofs / Appointment letter (with pay scale or CTC), Promotion letters (with revised scales or CTC), Form 16A and Salary slips (beginning and end of each employment)
  • Requisite caste / disability certificate (for SC/ST/Divyang – for fee waiver only)
  • Requisite Release certificate (if retired Armed Forces personnel)
  • CCA Declaration (applicable) and NOC at Final Personal Interview stage

Apply Online at: https://rac.gov.in

For queries: Phone 011-23889578 or email at [email protected] (for applying online); Phone 011-23889529 or email at [email protected] (for other queries).

Important Instructions for DRDO RAC Scientist Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • All candidates must apply online through https://rac.gov.in only. No other mode of application is accepted.
  • Closing Date: 19 June 2026 (1600 hrs.) - facility for online application submission will remain available on RAC website only up to this time.
  • Do not divulge any confidential information related to work experience of present or previous employer while filling the application; RAC shall not be held responsible for revelation of such information.
  • Ensure candidate's name and parents' names are filled exactly as in Date of Birth proof; name mismatch requires notarized affidavit at time of filling the online application.
  • For multiple posts - apply separately for each post and mention item numbers and order of preference in each application.
  • Do not change registered mobile number or Email after application - vital information about eligibility/shortlisting/selection status will be intimated through Email/SMS only.
  • Only locked/finalised applications shall be considered - no correction will be allowed after final submission. Preview application with all uploaded documents before final submission.
  • The maximum file size of each document/certificate should not exceed 500 KB; uploaded documents must be legible/readable and not password protected.
  • Final Personal Interview will be held in-person at RAC, Delhi only - no online/virtual interview mode will be considered under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must score minimum 75% marks (75 out of 100) in Final Personal Interview for consideration for selection.
  • Selected candidates will have liability to serve anywhere in India including remote/field area locations as per Central Government Rules.
  • Canvassing in any form will disqualify the candidate.
  • RAC does not request for any payment at any stage of recruitment process except the application fee. No such demand should be entertained.
  • Any dispute will be subject to the Hon'ble Courts/Tribunals having jurisdiction over Delhi only.
  • DRDO under DPDP Act 2023 declares that candidate's personal data received through online application will be processed for recruitment purpose only.
  • Incomplete applications will be summarily rejected.
  • The number of vacancies may vary; RAC reserves the right to modify the screening/shortlisting procedure depending upon availability of suitable candidates.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has published the Combined Graduate Level Examination Notification 2026 on 21 May 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 12,256 Group B and Group C posts across various Ministries and Departments. Candidates within the age range as specified in the official notification and holding a Graduation Degree are eligible to apply online at https://ssc.gov.in. The application process will be active from 21 May 2026 to 22 June 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

FOLLOW US

SSC CGL 2026 Notification PDF

Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Block No. 12, CGO Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003, has issued the official notice for Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 vide F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026.The notification includes complete information on vacancies, eligibility, age limits, reservation, examination scheme, syllabus, exam centres, application process, and candidate instructions. Applicants should read the official notice carefully before submitting the form.

  • Download SSC CGL 2026 Official Notification PDF

SSC CGL 2026 Overview

SSC is conducting Combined Graduate Level Examination 2026 for approximately 12,256 vacancies in Group B and Group C posts under different Ministries, Departments, and Government of India organizations. The application process is online only, and the last date to submit the application form is 22 June 2026.

Organization Name Staff Selection Commission (SSC), New Delhi
Examination Name Combined Graduate Level Examination, 2026 (SSC CGL 2026)
Advertisement No. F. No. HQ-C11018/1/2026-C-1 dated 21-05-2026
Post Name Various Group B (Gazetted/Non-Gazetted) and Group C posts including Assistant Section Officer, Inspector (IT/CE), Sub Inspector (CBI/NIA), Junior Statistical Officer, Assistant Audit Officer, Tax Assistant, Auditor, Accountant, Postal Assistant, and more
Total Vacancies Approximately 12,256 (tentative; updated vacancies will be on https://ssc.gov.in)
Pay Scale Pay Level 4 (Rs. 25,500–81,100) to Pay Level 8 (Rs. 47,600–1,51,100) depending on the post
Application Mode Online only at https://ssc.gov.in or via mY SSC mobile app
Official Website https://ssc.gov.in

SSC CGL 2026 – Eligibility Criteria

Criteria Details
Nationality Citizen of India, or subjects/persons of specified neighboring and migrated countries as permitted under Government of India rules, with eligibility certificate wherever required.
Essential Educational Qualification Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University or equivalent for most posts, as on 01-08-2026.
Junior Statistical Officer (JSO) Bachelor's Degree in any subject with at least 60% marks in Mathematics at 12th level; or Bachelor's Degree with Statistics as one of the subjects at degree level.
Statistical Investigator Grade-II Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Demography, Population Studies, Operation Research, IT, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Data Science, or Artificial Intelligence.
Assistant Audit Officer / Assistant Accounts Officer Bachelor's Degree is essential. Desirable qualifications include CA, CMA, CS, M.Com, MBA (Finance), or M.B.E.
Appearing Candidates Final year candidates may apply, but they must possess the essential qualification on or before 01-08-2026.
Distance Learning Degrees Accepted only if approved by Distance Education Bureau, UGC, for Central Government employment. Proof must be produced at document verification.
BRO Posts Only male candidates are eligible for vacancies reported in Border Roads Organization.

SSC CGL 2026 – Age Limit

Age will be calculated as on 01-08-2026. The date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or Secondary Examination Certificate will be treated as final.

SSC CGL 2026 – Application Fee

Category Fee
General / OBC / EWS (Male) Rs. 100/- (Non-Refundable)

SC / ST NIL (Exempted)
PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) NIL (Exempted)
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) eligible for reservation NIL (Exempted)

Fee must be paid online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or Visa, Mastercard, Maestro, and RuPay debit cards. Fee once paid will not be refunded, and applications without required fee payment will be rejected.

Correction Window Fee: Rs. 200/- for first correction and Rs. 500/- for second correction. This applies to all candidates, including fee-exempt applicants.

SSC CGL 2026 – Selection Process

Selection is through a two-tier Computer Based Examination (CBE). Marks scored in CBE conducted in multiple shifts will be normalized as per the procedure published by SSC vide Notice dated 02-06-2025. There is no provision for re-evaluation/re-checking of scores at any stage.

Tier-I: Computer Based Examination

Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Time
A. General Intelligence & Reasoning 25 50 1 hour (Sectional timer: 15 min per section)
1 hour 20 min for scribe-eligible PwBD candidates
B. General Awareness 25 50
C. Quantitative Aptitude 25 50
D. English Comprehension 25 50
Total 100 200  

Tier-I consists of Objective Type / MCQ questions, set in English and Hindi (except English Comprehension). Negative marking: 0.50 marks per wrong answer.

Tier-II: Computer Based Examination

Tier-II is conducted in Paper-I, Paper-II, and Paper-III in separate shifts/days.

Paper Section / Subject Questions Marks Duration
Paper-I (Compulsory for all posts) Section-I: Mathematical Abilities + Reasoning & General Intelligence 30+30 = 60 180 Session-I: 2 hrs 15 min; Section-I: 1 hr timer
Section-II: English Language & Comprehension + General Awareness 45+25 = 70 210 1 hr timer
Section-III: Computer Knowledge Test (qualifying, mandatory) 20 60 15 min
Section-IV: Data Entry Speed Test (DEST) (qualifying, mandatory) - - Session-II: 15 min
Paper-II (JSO / Statistical Investigator only) Statistics 100 200 2 hours
Paper-III (AAO / AAcO only) General Studies (Finance and Economics) 100 200 2 hours

Negative marking in Tier-II: 1 mark per wrong answer in Section-I, II, III of Paper-I; 0.50 marks in Paper-II and Paper-III. Section-III (CKT) and Section-IV (DEST) are qualifying in nature. Candidates must qualify all sections of Paper-I separately. Higher standards of CKT and DEST are set for specific posts (CSS, MEA, AFHQ, SFIO, CBIC, Postal etc.). Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available for PwBD candidates eligible for scribe.

SSC CGL 2026 – Scheme of Examination & Syllabus (Brief)

Paper / Section Key Topics
Tier-I: General Intelligence & Reasoning Analogies, similarities, space visualization, problem solving, arithmetic reasoning, number series, coding-decoding, Venn diagrams, embedded figures, critical thinking, emotional intelligence, social intelligence
Tier-I: General Awareness Current events, India and neighbouring countries, History, Culture, Geography, Economic Scene, General Policy, Scientific Research
Tier-I: Quantitative Aptitude Whole numbers, decimals, fractions, percentages, ratio, averages, SI/CI, profit & loss, discount, time & work, time & distance, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, bar charts, pie charts
Tier-I: English Comprehension Grammar, vocabulary, comprehension, writing ability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-I: Mathematical Abilities Number systems, arithmetic, algebra, geometry, mensuration, trigonometry, statistics & probability
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-II: English Language & Comprehension Vocabulary, grammar, synonyms, antonyms, idioms, sentence improvement, comprehension passages (current affairs + editorial based)
Tier-II Paper-I Sec-III: Computer Knowledge Computer basics, MS Office (Word/Excel/PowerPoint), Internet & e-mail, networking & cybersecurity basics
Tier-II Paper-II: Statistics Data collection, frequency distribution, central tendency, dispersion, correlation, regression, probability, sampling theory, statistical inference, ANOVA, time series, index numbers
Tier-II Paper-III: General Studies (Finance & Economics) Part A (Finance & Accounts, 80 marks): Financial accounting, GAAP, books of accounts, depreciation, bank reconciliation. Part B (Economics & Governance, 120 marks): Economic concepts, Indian economy, governance, financial markets, budget basics

SSC CGL 2026 – Exam Centres

The examination will be conducted through 9 SSC Regions covering all states and union territories. Candidates must choose three examination centres, in order of priority, within the same region.

Central Region: Agra, Lucknow, Prayagraj, Varanasi, Patna, Gaya Eastern Region: Kolkata, Ranchi, Bhubaneswar, Cuttack, Gangtok Karnataka Kerala Region: Bengaluru, Kochi, Kozhikode, Mysuru Madhya Pradesh Region: Bhopal, Indore, Jabalpur, Raipur North Eastern Region: Guwahati, Imphal, Shillong, Aizawl, Agartala Northern Region: Delhi, Jaipur, Dehradun North Western Region: Chandigarh, Shimla, Jammu, Srinagar, Amritsar Southern Region: Chennai, Hyderabad, Vijayawada, Coimbatore, Madurai Western Region: Mumbai, Pune, Ahmedabad, Surat, Nagpur, Panaji

No request for change of centre will be entertained after submission of the application. SSC may add, cancel, or shift candidates to another centre depending on administrative requirements.

Competition Level Analysis for SSC CGL 2026

The overall competition level for SSC CGL 2026 is expected to remain very high because the examination attracts a massive number of graduate applicants every year across India. Even with around 12,256 vacancies, the effective competition ratio remains extremely tough for high-demand posts.

  1. Applicant volume is huge: SSC CGL is among the most sought-after central government exams, and premium posts receive intense preference from top scorers.
  2. Tier-I acts as the main screening stage: With negative marking and sectional timing, both speed and accuracy matter. Candidates targeting top posts generally need a very strong Tier-I score.
  3. Tier-II gets post-specific: Specialized posts like JSO and AAO attract a smaller but more qualified pool, making domain preparation more important than raw competition numbers.
  4. DEST and CKT can eliminate candidates: Although qualifying in nature, poor performance in these sections can disqualify otherwise strong candidates for certain posts.
  5. Physical standards create an extra filter: Certain Inspector and Sub Inspector posts require physical and medical fitness standards that are checked separately by user departments.

Who Should NOT Apply for SSC CGL 2026?

  1. Candidates who will not possess a Bachelor's Degree by 01-08-2026.
  2. Candidates who fall outside the age limit for all eligible posts even after applicable relaxation.
  3. OBC Creamy Layer candidates intending to claim OBC reservation.
  4. Candidates who cannot meet post-specific physical or medical standards for the posts they plan to prefer.
  5. Non-matriculate Ex-Servicemen or those who have not completed the required length of service.
  6. Candidates applying for JSO without the required Mathematics or Statistics qualification.
  7. Female candidates applying specifically for BRO vacancies, where only male candidates are eligible.
  8. Distance-learning degree holders without valid UGC-DEB approval for the relevant period.

Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying SSC CGL 2026

  1. Not generating a fresh OTR on the new SSC website.
  2. Using a pre-existing photograph instead of live photo capture where required.
  3. Uploading blurred or wrongly sized signature files.
  4. Selecting the wrong reservation category without valid supporting certificate.
  5. Ignoring post-specific standards before locking post preferences.
  6. Choosing JSO or AAO related preferences without being ready for Paper-II or Paper-III.
  7. Skipping Aadhaar-based authentication even when eligible.
  8. Waiting until the final date for application submission.
  9. Missing the answer key objection window after examination.
  10. Confusing the correction window fee structure.

How to Apply for SSC CGL 2026

Candidates must apply online only at https://ssc.gov.in or through the mY SSC mobile application (available on Store). No other mode of application is accepted. Candidates who have not generated OTR on the new website must do so first. Once OTR is generated on the new website, it remains valid for all future examinations on that portal.

Candidates are advised to opt for Aadhaar-based authentication during OTR. Aadhaar-authenticated candidates get significant exemptions: their applications are not rejected for photograph/signature standards, and they need not carry photo ID at the examination venue.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Generate One-Time Registration (OTR) at https://ssc.gov.in (mandatory; use Aadhaar-based authentication if possible)
  2. Complete OTR profile and lock it
  3. Fill the Common Application Form (CAF) including live photograph capture via camera
  4. Upload scanned signature (JPEG/JPG, 10–20 KB, 6.0 cm × 2.0 cm)
  5. Fill the Examination Specific Module (post preferences, exam centre choices - 3 centres within same region in order of priority)
  6. Pay application fee of Rs. 100/- online (if applicable) by 23-06-2026
  7. Preview/Print the application to verify all details before final submission
  8. Use the correction window (29-06 to 01-07-2026) if any correction is needed (chargeable)
  9. Keep printout of application and all documents for at least 3 years

Documents to Keep Ready for Document Verification (after final selection):

  • Matriculation/Secondary School Certificate (proof of date of birth)
  • Mark sheets for all years/semesters of Graduation + Provisional/Degree Certificate
  • SC/ST Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure of the notification)
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format (Annexure-X); must be valid as on closing date of application
  • EWS Income & Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026–27 (issued based on income for FY 2025–26) in prescribed format (Annexure-XI)
  • PwBD Disability Certificate from competent authority specifying type of disability (as per Annexure-XII to XIII)
  • Ex-Serviceman discharge certificate and other relevant ESM documents
  • Equivalence certificate (for equivalent qualifications other than standard degrees)
  • DEB/UGC approval letter (for Distance Learning degree holders)
  • Certificate of proficiency in State Regional/official language (for AAO/Assistant Audit Officer State Cadre)

Apply Online at: https://ssc.gov.in | Toll Free Helpline: 1800 309 3063

Important Instructions for SSC CGL 2026 Candidates

  • Final allotment is based on merit-cum-preference, and no alternate post will be offered if a candidate fails post-specific standards after allotment.
  • OBC candidates claiming reservation must not belong to the creamy layer as on the closing date of application.
  • EWS certificate must be valid for FY 2026–27 and should be based on income for FY 2025–26.
  • PwBD candidates must choose the correct sub-category in the application form because no later change is allowed.
  • Scribe rules are strict: the scribe must complete OTR, be Aadhaar-authenticated, and cannot assist more than one candidate.
  • Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour is available to eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Tentative answer keys can be challenged online by paying Rs. 100 per question within the specified period only.
  • No re-evaluation or re-checking of scores is available at any stage.
  • Candidates should preserve the application form and related documents for at least 3 years.
  • Candidature remains provisional until certificates and documents are verified by the Appointing Authority.
  • Fraudulent claims of reservation status may lead to debarment from all SSC examinations.
  • Any update in exam schedule or process will be communicated through the SSC website only.
  • Applications without the required fee, unless exempted, or incomplete applications will be summarily rejected.

The latest Certification Engineers International Limited (CEIL) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for multiple technical and non-technical Fixed Term Contract posts! Published on the official CEIL website, this recruitment drive offers 85 openings across QA/QC Engineering, Safety, Marketing, SCM, Office Management, and HR functions. Eligible candidates who meet the age and experience criteria as of 30 April 2026 can apply online from the CEIL portal starting immediately up to 15 June 2026. Find all details about the CEIL Recruitment 2026, including eligibility, online application process, and official notification PDF here.

CEIL Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Certification Engineers International Limited (CEIL), Head Office, Navi Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CEIL/HR&A/Advt./2026-27/01 for engagement of multiple technical and non-technical personnel on Fixed Term Contract basis. The notification includes complete details on post-wise vacancies, eligibility, experience requirements, salary, reservation breakup, selection process, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official CEIL website.

  • Download CEIL Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

CEIL Recruitment 2026 Overview

CEIL Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates must meet the educational qualification and post-qualification experience requirements as on the cut-off date 30.04.2026. Prescribed educational qualifications and experience are the minimum qualifying criteria - mere possession does not entitle candidates for interview.

Position Educational Qualification Field of Experience
Officer Grade I to IV (QA/QC – Mechanical / Production / Civil / Electrical & Instrumentation) Engineering Graduate or Diploma in Mechanical / Production / Civil / Electrical discipline with minimum 50% marks Relevant experience in Third Party Inspection / Certification of Offshore, Refineries, Cross Country Pipelines, fabricated equipment, rotating equipment, mechanical bulk items, electrical & instrumentation equipment, CP System, civil infrastructure and railways projects. Additional: For Mechanical/Production – ASNT Level-II in NDT Methods (UT, RT, MPT, PT) is mandatory; ASNT Level-III / CSWIP 3.1 / PAUT preferred. For E&I – NACE Level-I / Level-II preferred.
Officer Grade-II [Mech – Offshore (Deep Sea)] Engineering Graduate or Diploma in Mechanical / Production 6 years for Degree / 12 years for Diploma. Awareness of international codes and standards (ASTM, NACE, AWS, ASNT, BS, API 5L, API 17J, ISO, DNV, etc.). Experience of inspecting fabrication and installation of piping, piping components as per API, ASTM, ASME, BS, NACE codes. Experience in AUT (Automated UT).
Officer Grade-II [E&I – Offshore (Deep Sea)] BE / Diploma in Electrical & Instrumentation Minimum 6 years for Degree / 12 years for Diploma. Awareness of international codes (API, NEMA, NEC, IEC, IEEE, BIS, BS, ASTM, ISO, OSHA, etc.). Experience in Umbilicals, FAT and SAT of instruments, DCS, SCADA, PLC's, HIPPS, Telecommunication systems, etc.
Officer Grade-III (Safety Officer) Engineering Graduate in any branch with minimum 50% marks AND Degree / Diploma in Industrial Safety Relevant experience in Safety field; process safety experience preferred for deployment in process facilities.
Officer Grade-II (Marketing & Business Development) Engineering Graduate or Diploma in Mechanical / Production / Civil / IT / CS / Electrical / Electronics / Electronics & Telecommunication / Instrumentation with minimum 50% marks Relevant experience with responsibility of sales, marketing and tendering / bidding activities in Consultancy and / or EPC business.
Officer Grade-II (Contract & Purchase / SCM) Engineering Graduate or Diploma in Mechanical / Production / Civil / IT / CS / Electrical / Electronics / Electronics & Telecommunication / Instrumentation with minimum 50% marks Relevant experience in managing and uploading / data entry of documents in EDMS/EPS systems, GeM Portal, CPP Portal, file maintenance, managing appointments and meetings, liaison for meetings/conferences, and other office related tasks.
Officer Grade-II (Office Management) Engineering Graduate or Diploma in Mechanical / Production / Civil / IT / CS / Electrical / Electronics / Electronics & Telecommunication / Instrumentation with minimum 50% marks Relevant experience with good communication skills (written and oral), Computer knowledge in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), hands-on experience of secretarial works to MD / Director / Owner / Higher official, preparation of presentations. Should have experience in managing the CEO's calendar end-to-end and planning domestic and international travel itineraries as per PSU norms.
HR Associate Grade-II Graduate in any discipline with Diploma in Personnel Management with minimum 50% marks Minimum 5 years' experience in handling various aspects of Personnel / Administrative functions.

CEIL Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age is calculated as on 30.04.2026. Upper age limits are as per designation and discipline, detailed in the vacancy table above. Relaxations are applicable as per Government of India guidelines.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) / EWS As per maximum age indicated for each post (35–55 years depending on grade and discipline)
SC / ST Relaxation as per Govt. of India guidelines; Experience also relaxable at discretion of CEIL
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Relaxation as per Govt. of India guidelines
PwD (Persons with Disabilities) Upper age limit relaxable by 10 years; Persons with 40% or more disability are eligible
Ex-Servicemen Upper age limit is relaxable as per Govt. of India guidelines

CEIL Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

The notification does not mention any application fee. Applications are to be submitted online free of charge through CEIL's official recruitment portal at https://www6.eil.co.in/ceilrecruitment/.

CEIL Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Applications received will be put up to a Committee for shortlisting. Screening and selection will be based on the details provided in the application form - only accurate, full and correct information should be furnished. Furnishing of wrong/false information will result in disqualification.

For Officer Grade Posts (QA/QC, Safety, Marketing, SCM, Office Management): Shortlisting based on application details, followed by Personal Interview. Interviews will be held at CEIL office, Navi Mumbai. Online interviews (Skype, Google Meet, or similar) may also be conducted if required.

For HR Associate Category: Selection will be done through Written Test. Candidates complying with the criteria will be shortlisted for the written test.

CEIL may restrict the number of candidates called for interview / written test based on academic excellence, age, and experience. Selected candidates will be on panel and appointment letters will be issued as per requirement.

CEIL Recruitment 2026 - Salary & Benefits

Designation Consolidated Salary Per Month
Officer Grade-I ₹53,625/-
Officer Grade-II ₹66,300/-
Officer Grade-III ₹80,925/-
Officer Grade-IV ₹94,195/-
HR Associate Grade-II ₹41,821/-

Section 1: QA/QC Engineers (Third Party Inspection / Certification Engineers)

Position Place of Posting Consolidated Salary/Month (Rs.)*
QA/QC Engineer – Mechanical / Production Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹53,625
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹66,300
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹80,925
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹94,195
QA/QC Engineer – Mech – Offshore (Deep Sea) Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹66,300
QA/QC Engineer – Electrical & Instrumentation Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹53,625
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹66,300
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹80,925
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹94,195
QA/QC Engineer – E&I – Offshore (Deep Sea) Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹66,300
QA/QC Engineer – Civil Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹53,625
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹66,300
Project Sites / CEIL offices ₹80,925
Safety Officer Mumbai ₹80,925

Section 2: Marketing & Business Development, Contract & Purchase, Office Management, HR Associate

Position Designation Place of Posting Consolidated Salary/Month (Rs.)*
Marketing & Business Development Officer Officer Grade-II Mumbai office ₹66,300
Contract & Purchase (SCM) Officer Officer Grade-II Mumbai office ₹66,300
Office Management Officer Officer Grade-II Mumbai office ₹66,300
HR Associate HR Associate Grade-II Mumbai office ₹41,821

Other benefits include: annual increment @ 3% on consolidated salary (for extensions beyond 1 year, applicable for up to next 2 years); Medical Insurance of minimum ₹2,00,000/- for self - premium reimbursed by CEIL; Medical Fitness Certificate expense reimbursable at time of joining. No TA/DA for attending interviews. No other perks or allowances beyond consolidated salary. Company's PF contribution is part of the consolidated salary. HRA component in salary may vary based on city of posting classification (calculated considering 'X' class city HRA).

Who should NOT apply for CEIL Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates who do not hold an Engineering Degree or Diploma in the relevant discipline with minimum 50% marks - this is a hard requirement for all technical posts.
  • Mechanical/Production QA/QC candidates who do not have ASNT Level-II certification in NDT methods (UT, RT, MPT, PT) - this is mandatory and non-negotiable.
  • Candidates who do not have the prescribed minimum post-qualification experience as on 30.04.2026 - experience counted is post-PQ only.
  • Candidates exceeding the maximum age limit for their respective grade and discipline (Degree or Diploma holder category) as on 30.04.2026.
  • Candidates looking for a permanent government job - this is a Fixed Term Contract with no liability on CEIL for regular appointment at any stage; total contract period will not exceed 36 months under any circumstances.
  • Candidates not willing to serve anywhere in India - posting can be at any CEIL office or project site across India at the discretion of management.
  • OBC candidates from creamy layer - only OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates are eligible for reservation benefits.
  • PwD candidates with less than 40% disability - minimum 40% disability is required for PwD relaxation benefits.
  • Candidates without a valid and functional email ID that can remain active for at least 24 months from date of application - all communications will be via email only.
  • Candidates for the Safety Officer post without a Degree/Diploma in Industrial Safety in addition to the engineering degree - both are mandatory.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying for CEIL Recruitment 2026?

  • Not checking ASNT Level-II certification status before applying for Mechanical/Production QA/QC posts - it is mandatory and absence will disqualify the application.
  • Miscalculating experience years - experience must be post-qualification (after engineering degree/diploma) and is counted as on 30.04.2026, not the date of application.
  • Applying with CGPA/OGPA without converting to equivalent percentage - candidates must indicate the equivalent percentage as per university/institute norms and be ready to produce documentary proof at interview.
  • Applying through any mode other than CEIL's official recruitment portal (https://www6.eil.co.in/ceilrecruitment/) - applications through any other mode will not be entertained.
  • Providing an incorrect or inactive email ID - all communications including interview/test intimation will be via email only; CEIL will not be responsible for loss of communication due to invalid email.
  • Applying for Grade-II posts in Marketing, SCM, or Office Management without adequate experience in those specific functional areas - engineering qualification alone is not sufficient; relevant functional experience is mandatory.
  • Not keeping track of CEIL's official website www.ceil.co.in - any change in last date or other schedule will be communicated only through the website, not separately.
  • Attending the interview without carrying all original documents - absence of any required document (age proof, marksheets, experience certificates, salary slip, category certificate) will result in disqualification from interview.
  • Canvassing in any form - this will immediately result in disqualification.
  • Not preparing 5 copies of the Annexure (CV format) - shortlisted candidates will need to bring 5 copies of the Annexure, which will be sent only to shortlisted candidates by CEIL.

How to Apply for CEIL Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online only through CEIL's official recruitment portal. Applications received through any other mode shall not be entertained. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date. Applications found incomplete or received after the due date are liable to be rejected.

Documents Required at the time of Interview:

  • Proof of Age (10th / Matriculation certificate)
  • Educational and Professional Qualifications (all marksheets & certificates)
  • Service Certificate of past employment, proof of date of joining & continuity in present organization (if applicable) & latest salary slip
  • Medical Certificate for PwD (if applicable) with photograph from competent authority, confirming minimum 40% disability
  • SC / ST / OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) certificate from competent authority (OBC certificate must clearly state non-creamy layer status)
  • EWS certificate from competent authority
  • 5 copies of duly filled Annexure / CV (format sent to shortlisted candidates only)

Apply Online at: https://www6.eil.co.in/ceilrecruitment/

For further updates: Visit www.ceil.co.in → Careers section

Important Instructions for CEIL Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • This is a Fixed Term Contract engagement - purely on a temporary basis. It does not carry any liability on CEIL for regular appointment at any stage. Total contract period will not exceed 36 months under any circumstances.
  • Initial appointment is for one year, extendable (if required) for another 36 months or till completion of the project, whichever is earlier, subject to satisfactory performance.
  • Selected candidates will initially be posted at CEIL Head Office, Navi Mumbai but may be posted at any CEIL office or project site anywhere in India at management's discretion. Only candidates willing to serve anywhere in India need apply.
  • No perks or allowances other than the consolidated salary are payable. In case of extension beyond one year, increment @ 3% per year will be considered for the next 2 years.
  • Fixed Term Personnel must take Medical Insurance of minimum ₹2,00,000/- for self; the premium will be reimbursed by CEIL.
  • CGPA/OGPA/CPI or letter grade in Degree must be converted to equivalent percentage as per university/institute norms and documentary proof will be required at interview.
  • No TA/DA will be given for attending interviews.
  • Candidates must be of sound health; Medical Fitness Certificate from Government Hospital / Company Nominated Hospital is required at the time of joining (expense reimbursable on original receipt).
  • Contractual engagement may be terminated by CEIL at any time at its sole discretion without assigning any reason if performance/conduct is found unsatisfactory.
  • For termination during currency of contract, one month's written notice or one month's consolidated pay in lieu thereof shall be given by either party.
  • Reservation and relaxation for PwDs shall be as per GOI guidelines. Persons with 40% or more disabilities are eligible.
  • Experience prescribed is relaxable at the discretion of CEIL for SC/ST candidates.
  • Canvassing in any form will result in disqualification.
  • Number of vacancies indicated are only tentative; CEIL reserves the right to cancel/restrict/modify/alter selection/recruitment process at any stage without notice.
  • CEIL also reserves the right to reject any candidature without assigning any reason whatsoever.
  • Any dispute arising from this recruitment will be under the jurisdiction of Mumbai court only.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Key Highlights

FOLLOW US
  • Vacancy: EdCIL (India) Ltd. is recruiting 879 Multidisciplinary Staff and PMU Consultant positions under the Inclusive Education–PM SHRI project.
  • Qualification: Prescribed qualifications from recognized institutions with required registration/licensing and experience, depending on the post.
  • Last Date: Online applications can be submitted until 04 June 2026.
  • Salary: Monthly remuneration ranges from Rs. 25,000/- to Rs. 55,000/- depending on the post.
  • Selection: Shortlisting based on academic/professional qualifications and Interview.
  • Job Type: Purely contractual basis.
  • Apply Mode: Online only through the EdCIL recruitment portal.

EdCIL (India) Ltd. (A Mini Ratna Category-I CPSE, Government of India) has published the Multidisciplinary Staff Notification 2026 on 22 May 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 879 Multidisciplinary Staff and PMU Consultant positions. Candidates within the age range of up to 45 years and holding the prescribed qualifications from recognized institutions are eligible to apply online at the official EdCIL recruitment portal. The application process will be active from 22 May 2026 to 04 June 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Notification PDF

EdCIL (India) Ltd has published the official Recruitment Notification for engagement of Multidisciplinary Staff and PMU Consultants for Autism Centres in Andhra Pradesh under Inclusive Education–PM SHRI. The notification includes post-wise vacancies, remuneration, educational qualifications, experience, age limits, selection process, general instructions, and the online application link. Candidates are strongly advised to read the complete advertisement before applying.

  • Download EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Notification PDF

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Overview

EdCIL is recruiting Multidisciplinary Staff and PMU Consultants for Autism Centres across Andhra Pradesh, with the closing date 04 June 2026.

Organization Name EdCIL (India) Ltd. (A Mini Ratna Category-I CPSE, Government of India)
Advertisement No. TSG/Autism/Multidisciplinary-Team/2026-01
Project / Scheme Inclusive Education – PM SHRI, Andhra Pradesh (Autism Centres)
Post Names Occupational Therapist, Physiotherapist, Psychologist, Speech Therapist, Clinical Psychologist, Special Educator (Secondary Level), Special Educator (Primary Level), PMU Consultant
Total Vacancies 875 Multidisciplinary Staff + 4 PMU Consultants
Engagement Type Purely contractual basis
Workplace Autism Centres across various Municipalities of Andhra Pradesh as approved by SPD (Inclusive Education – PM SHRI, Andhra Pradesh)
Upper Age Limit 45 years for all posts (as on 30 April 2026)
Application Mode Online only through recruitment portal
Selection Mode Shortlisting based on academic/professional qualifications and Interview
Application Fee No application fee required
Official Recruitment Portal https://tsgrecruitment.edcil.co.in/login

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Date of Advertisement 22 May 2026
Cut-off Date for Age and Experience 30 April 2026
Last Date for Submission of Online Application 04 June 2026

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 – Post-wise Vacancy & Remuneration

EdCIL is hiring for 879 Multidisciplinary Staff including Psychologist, Speech Therapist, Clinical Psychologist, PMU Consultant and Occupational Therapist. The last date is 04 June 2026.

S.No. Post Name No. of Posts Remuneration (per month)
1 Occupational Therapist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
2 Physiotherapist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
3 Psychologist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
4 Speech Therapist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
5 Clinical Psychologist 125 Rs. 30,000/-
6 Special Educator (Secondary Level) 125 Rs. 30,000/-
7 Special Educator (Primary Level) 125 Rs. 25,000/-
8 PMU Consultant 4 Rs. 55,000/-

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 – Educational Qualifications

Candidates must possess the prescribed qualifications from Government/RCI recognized institutions and fulfil the eligibility criteria as on 30 April 2026.

Occupational Therapist (125 Posts)

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Occupational Therapy from a recognized institution.
  • Valid registration/license to practise as an Occupational Therapist in India, as per applicable state regulations.
  • Strong clinical reasoning and problem-solving abilities, excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team, with a compassionate, patient-centred approach.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Physiotherapist (125 Posts)

  • Bachelor’s / Master’s Degree in Physiotherapy from a recognized institution.
  • Valid professional registration/license.
  • Strong clinical reasoning and communication skills, compassionate approach, ability to work independently and in a team.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Psychologist (125 Posts)

  • M.A./M.Sc in Psychology from a recognized university (Clinical/Counselling/Developmental Psychology preferred).
  • Experience in psychological assessments and behavioural therapies.
  • Strong communication, counselling and interpersonal skills, ability to work independently and as part of a team.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Speech Therapist (125 Posts)

  • Bachelor’s / Master’s Degree in Speech-Language Pathology or Audiology & Speech-Language Pathology from a recognized institution.
  • Mandatory valid registration with the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI).
  • Experience working with children with Autism and developmental disorders preferred.
  • Strong assessment, communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Proficiency in Telugu and English (knowledge of other local languages is an added advantage).
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team; experienced candidates will be given added advantage.

Clinical Psychologist (125 Posts)

  • Post Graduation in Clinical Psychology from an institution recognized by the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI).
  • Mandatory valid RCI registration.
  • Strong expertise in psychological assessments and behavioural therapies.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills; proficiency in Telugu is an added advantage.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team; experienced candidates will be given added advantage.

Special Educator (Secondary Level) – 125 Posts

  • The candidate must possess any one of the following RCI-recognised qualifications with valid CRR number:
    • Bachelor’s Degree + B.Ed. (Special Education), OR
    • Bachelor’s Degree + B.Ed. (General) with 1-year Diploma in Autism/Intellectual Disability, OR
    • Bachelor’s Degree + B.Ed. (General) with D.Ed. in Autism/Intellectual Disability.
  • Qualified AP TET (Paper-2B mandatory) / CTET in Special Education.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

Special Educator (Primary Level) – 125 Posts

  • Must have passed Intermediate (10+2) from a recognized Board.
  • Must possess a two-year Diploma in Special Education (Autism/Intellectual Disability) recognized by RCI with valid CRR number.
  • Qualified AP TET (Paper-1B mandatory) / CTET in Special Education.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.

PMU Consultant (4 Posts)

  • Ph.D./M.Phil in Clinical Psychology/Psychology/Special Education.
  • Desirable: Ph.D in the field of rehabilitation of Persons with Disabilities.
  • Experienced candidates in the relevant field will be given added advantage.
  • Expertise in report writing and strong communication skills is mandatory.

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 - Age Limit

S.No. Post Name Upper Age Limit
1 Occupational Therapist 45 years
2 Physiotherapist 45 years
3 Psychologist 45 years
4 Speech Therapist 45 years
5 Clinical Psychologist 45 years
6 Special Educator (Secondary Level) 45 years
7 Special Educator (Primary Level) 45 years
8 PMU Consultant 45 years

EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026 – General Instructions

  • Only online applications will be accepted; only Indian Nationals are eligible.
  • The workplace will be Autism Centres across various Municipalities of Andhra Pradesh as approved by SPD (Inclusive Education–PM SHRI, Andhra Pradesh).
  • The competent authority reserves the right to amend, cancel or change the advertisement wholly or partly without assigning any reason.
  • The engagement will not confer any right for regular engagement, permanent absorption or continued contractual engagement.
  • Qualifications must be from Government recognized institutions only.
  • Candidates should have strong verbal and written communication skills and excellent working knowledge of computers (MS Word, MS Excel, MS PowerPoint etc.) and presentation skills.
  • Selection will be based on academic/professional qualifications and may include an interview on a prescribed date and time as decided by the competent authority.
  • A waiting list of candidates will be prepared for possible future vacancies or non-joining of selected candidates.
  • Original documents to prove qualifications, age, experience, etc., must be produced at the time of interview.
  • The cut-off date for age and experience is 30 April 2026; eligibility must be satisfied as on this date.
  • Candidates must upload all required documents (educational certificates, resume, experience certificates etc.) in PDF format only; failure will result in disqualification/rejection.
  • Screening and shortlisting will strictly be based on details and documents provided in the online application; incomplete, false or misrepresented information will lead to disqualification.
  • Any false declaration or suppression of information detected at any stage will lead to cancellation of candidature/termination of services and may attract disciplinary/legal action.
  • Interview letters and related communication will be sent only to shortlisted candidates via e-mail as provided in the online application; candidates must keep their email active.
  • Merely fulfilling eligibility criteria does not entitle a candidate to be called for interview.
  • Engagement is purely contractual; extension of contract depends on the needs of Inclusive Education–PM SHRI, Andhra Pradesh and the performance of the professional.
  • The last date for receipt of online applications is 04 June 2026.
  • No application fee is required for any of the posts.

How to Apply for EdCIL Multidisciplinary Staff 2026

Candidates must apply online only; no other mode of application will be accepted.

  1. Visit the online recruitment portal at https://tsgrecruitment.edcil.co.in/login.
  2. Register and fill in the online application form with accurate and complete details of personal information, qualifications and experience.
  3. Upload all required documents such as educational certificates, experience certificates, resume and other supporting documents in PDF format only.
  4. Verify all details before final submission, as screening and shortlisting will be based only on the information and documents submitted.
  5. Submit the online application on or before 04 June 2026.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Key Highlights

FOLLOW US
  • Vacancy: Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL) is hiring 20 Project Engineers across Electronics (10), Mechanical (5), and Computer Science (5) disciplines.
  • Qualification: First Class BE/B.Tech in the relevant discipline with minimum 1 year post-qualification experience.
  • Interview Date: Walk-in Interview on 6th & 7th June 2026, reporting at 09:00 AM (last entry 02:00 PM).
  • Salary: ₹30,000/- per month in the first year, rising up to ₹39,000/- by the fourth year.
  • Age Limit: Maximum 28 years for UR/EWS candidates as on 07 May 2026.
  • Selection: Direct Walk-in Interview; no online application.
  • Job Type: Temporary/Contract (initially 1 year, extendable up to 4 years).

Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL) has released the BDL Recruitment 2026 Notification (Advt No. BDL/C-HR (TA & CP)/2026-1) on 27th May 2026 at the official website bdl-india.in. A total of 20 vacancies have been announced for the post of Project Engineer across Electronics (10 posts), Mechanical (5 posts), and Computer Science (5 posts) disciplines. The selection will be through a Walk-in Interview on 6th and 7th June 2026 at The Aeronautical Society of India (AeSI), Bengaluru. The upper age limit is 28 years for UR/EWS candidates as on 07.05.2026. Monthly salary starts at ₹30,000/- in the first year, rising up to ₹39,000/- by the fourth year. Candidates must hold a First Class BE/B.Tech in the relevant discipline with a minimum of 1 year of post-qualification experience.

BDL Recruitment 2026 - Notification OUT

Bharat Dynamics Limited, a Miniratna Category-I Public Sector Enterprise under the Ministry of Defence, Government of India, has officially released its recruitment notification for Project Engineers on a temporary (contract) basis. The place of posting will be BDL - Project Office, ADE, Suranjan Das Road, New Tippasandra, Bengaluru, though candidates may be transferred to any BDL unit across India.

Download BDL Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

BDL Project Engineer 2026 - Vacancy Details

BDL has announced a total of 20 Project Engineer vacancies across 3 disciplines - Electronics (10), Mechanical (5), and Computer Science (5) - with reservations for SC, ST, OBC (NCL), and EWS candidates.

Post Name Total
Project Engineer (Electronics) 10

Project Engineer (Computer Science) 05
Total 20

BDL Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates

The BDL Walk-in Interview is scheduled for 6th and 7th June 2026. Candidates must reach the venue by 09:00 AM; no entry will be permitted after 02:00 PM on interview days.

Event Date
Official Notification Release 27 May 2026
Age Reckoned As On 07 May 2026
Walk-in Interview Dates 6th June 2026 & 7th June 2026
Reporting Time 09:00 AM onwards
Last Entry Deadline 02:00 PM (strictly)

How to Apply for BDL Recruitment 2026?

BDL Project Engineer 2026 is a Walk-in Interview - there is no online application form. Candidates must appear directly at the venue with all required documents. Follow these steps:

  1. Download the official BDL Recruitment 2026 notification PDF from bdl-india.in → HR section.
  2. Download and print the Bio-data Form (Annexure-I) attached to the notification.
  3. Fill the Bio-data Form in capital letters, affix a recent passport-size colour photograph, and self-attest all required documents.
  4. Calculate your age as on 07.05.2026 and verify that you meet the upper age limit for your category.
  5. Arrange one set of self-attested photocopies of all required documents (originals must also be carried for verification).
  6. Reach The Aeronautical Society of India (AeSI), Suranjan Das Road, New Tippasandra, Bengaluru - 560075 (near Vivekananda Metro Station) by 09:00 AM on the interview date.
  7. Submit your Bio-data Form and documents at the registration desk. Documents will be scrutinised to verify eligibility.
  8. Eligible candidates will be called for the interview on the same day or a later day (candidates may need to stay more than one day at their own expense).
  9. No TA or DA will be paid. Mobile phones and electronic devices are not permitted inside the venue.

Queries: Email [email protected] with subject line: Query-BDL/Walk-in/2026-1

BDL Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

There is no application fee for BDL Project Engineer Walk-in Interview 2026. No TA or DA will be paid to candidates attending the walk-in interview.

Category Application Fee
All Categories (UR / EWS / OBC / SC / ST / PwBD) Nil (No Fee)
Travelling / Dearness Allowance for Interview Not Applicable (self-funded)

BDL Project Engineer 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

What is the Age Limit for BDL Project Engineer 2026?

The upper age limit for BDL Project Engineer 2026 is reckoned as on 07.05.2026. Age relaxation is available as per Government of India rules.

Category Upper Age Limit
UR / EWS 28 years
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 31 years (+3 years relaxation)
SC / ST 33 years (+5 years relaxation)
PwBD (over & above category relaxation) +10 years additional relaxation
Ex-Servicemen Military service period + 3 years
J&K Domicile (01.01.1980-31.12.1989) +5 years relaxation

What is the Educational Qualification for BDL Project Engineer 2026?

Candidates must hold a First Class (60%) BE / B.Tech / B.Sc Engg (4 years) / Integrated M.E. / M.Tech in the relevant discipline from an AICTE-approved institution. For SC/ST candidates applying against reserved posts, 55% marks are sufficient.

Post Equivalent Disciplines Accepted
Project Engineer (Electronics) Electronics Engg; Electronics & Communication Engg; Electronics & Instrumentation Engg; Electronics Design & Technology Engg; Applied Electronics Engg; Electronics & Telecommunication Engg; Electronics & Control Engg
Project Engineer (Mechanical) Mechanical Engg; Industrial & Production Engg; Mechanical Production & Tool Engg; Production Engg; Production Technology Manufacturing Engg; Production & Industrial Engg; Manufacturing Technology; Aerospace Engg; Aeronautical Engg
Project Engineer (Computer Science) Computer Science Engineering

No rounding off allowed (59.99% is not treated as 60%). Academic / Teaching / Internship / Project Work experience will NOT count as industrial experience.

What is the Experience Requirement for BDL Project Engineer 2026?

All posts require a minimum of 1 year of post-qualification industrial experience in the relevant domain. Key areas per discipline:

Post Required Experience Areas
Electronics Embedded systems, RF, Power Electronics, Schematic / Troubleshooting, system design, LabVIEW, Control systems, FPGA, C, C++, VC++, Software development, weapon checkout system
Mechanical CAD (SolidWorks, AutoCAD, CATIA, MATLAB), FEA, Thermodynamics, Fluid Mechanics, Heat Transfer, Material Science, 3D Modelling & Simulation, Product Development, Machine Design, Quality Control, ASME/ISO compliance
Computer Science Application Software development for weapon systems; FPGA/VHDL, C, C++, VC++, Python, ADA, HILLS, Dspace, NI Veristand, ETAS, LABCAR, Opal-RT; Testing tools: LDRA, POLYSPACE, DO178STDS, OPEL RT (RTLAB)

BDL Project Engineer 2026 - Salary

BDL Project Engineer salary starts at ₹30,000/- per month in the first year and increases to ₹39,000/- per month by the fourth year, plus an annual allowance of ₹10,000/- for medical, attire, and footwear expenses.

Post 1st Year 2nd Year 3rd Year 4th Year
Project Engineer (All Disciplines) ₹30,000/- pm ₹33,000/- pm ₹36,000/- pm ₹39,000/- pm

In addition, ₹10,000/- per year is paid in two instalments towards medical insurance premium, attire allowance, stitching charges, and footwear allowance. The contract is initially for 1 year, extendable up to a maximum of 4 years based on organisational requirement and individual performance.

BDL Project Engineer 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for BDL Project Engineer 2026 is based on a total of 100 marks across three components. The minimum qualifying marks are 60% (with relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD as per Government rules).

  • 75 Academic Marks - (1 mark per % of degree, max 75)
  • 10 Post-Qualification Experience (1yr=3 pts · 2yr=6 pts · 3yr+=10 pts)
  • 15 Walk-in Interview Performance

Documents Required for BDL Walk-in Interview 2026

Candidates must bring one set of self-attested photocopies along with all original documents for verification at the walk-in interview venue.

  • Duly filled Bio-data Form (Annexure-I) with recent passport-size colour photograph
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Proof of Date of Birth / SSC Certificate / Matriculation Marksheet
  • Intermediate / XII / Diploma Certificate & Marks Memo(s)
  • BE / B.Tech / B.Sc Engg. Certificate & Marks Memo(s)
  • Category Certificate (SC / ST / OBC-NCL / EWS / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen) in prescribed Government of India format
  • Experience Certificate indicating date of joining and date of leaving / Offer of Appointment + Last 3 months' Pay Slips
  • Mandatory Salary Proof: Bank statement showing monthly salary credit / PF Statement / ESI Statement for the claimed experience period
  • Percentage equivalent certificate or specialisation certificate (if CGPA / no specialisation mentioned in degree)
  • OBC (NCL) Certificate issued on or after 01-04-2026
  • EWS Income & Asset Certificate valid for FY 2026-27 (based on FY 2025-26 verification)
