📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Central Bank of India (CBI) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Assistant General Manager (AGM) - Specialist Category posts! Published on 30 April 2026 at www.centralbank.bank.in, this new recruitment drive offers 15 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 33 to 45 years age group and possessing qualifications such as CA/CFA/CMA, MBA/PGDM, B.E./B.Tech, PG degree/diploma, or relevant specialist certification with required experience can apply online from 30 April 2026 to 07 June 2026. Find all details about the CBI AGM Specialist new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Central Bank of India, a leading Public Sector Bank with PAN India branch network of more than 4500 branches and total business of more than Rs. 8,12,000 Crores, has officially released a Recruitment Process Notification for selection of 15 Assistant General Manager (AGM) in Specialist Category in Risk Management, Finance & Accounts and Credit (Scale V). The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility criteria, educational qualification, experience, age limit, selection process, examination pattern, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the official Central Bank of India website.

  • Download Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Central Bank of India (Human Capital Management - Recruitment & Promotion, Central Office)
Post Name Assistant General Manager (AGM) - Specialist Category
Scale Scale V (Senior Management Grade)
Specializations Risk Management, Compliance/Risk Management, Asset Liability Management, Digital Risk & Analytics, Finance & Accounts, Credit Officer
Total Vacancies 15
Pay Scale Basic: Rs. 1,20,940 - 3360/2 - 1,27,660 - 3680/2 - 1,35,020/-

Official Website https://www.centralbank.bank.in

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 15 vacancies have been announced for the post of Assistant General Manager (Scale V) in Specialist Category. These are distributed across SC, ST, OBC, EWS and UR categories as follows. The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary according to actual bank requirement. Reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD (Divyangjan) categories is applicable as per Government of India guidelines.

Sr. No. Post Scale Total
1 Risk Management
i Risk Management V 2
ii Compliance / Risk Management 1
iii Asset Liability Management 1
iv Digital Risk & Analytics 1
2 Finance & Accounts 5
3 Credit Officer 5
Total 15

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 31-03-2026. All educational qualifications must be from a university recognized by Govt. of India / approved by Govt. Regulatory Bodies. Any experience of less than 6 months in an organization shall not be considered for determining eligibility, except when such experience is part of current employment. The post-wise educational qualification and experience requirements are as follows:

Post-wise Educational Qualification, Experience and Age Limit

Post Name Essential Educational Qualification Additional/Mandatory Certification Experience
AGM - Risk Management (Scale V) Full-time B.Sc in Statistics/Economics / Bachelor's in Analytics (Statistics, Applied Maths, OR/Data Science) with 55% marks OR Full-time MBA Finance/Banking or PGDBM/IPGD-Risk Management with 55% marks - from AICTE/UGC approved University. Mandatory: FRM (GARP USA) / CFA (CFA Institute USA) / PRM (PRMIA USA). Preferable: Certification in SPSS/SAS, R, Python. Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.
AGM - Compliance / Risk Management (Scale V) Full-time B.Sc in Statistics/Economics / Bachelor's in Analytics with 55% marks OR Full-time MBA Finance/Banking or PGDBM/IPGD-Risk Management with 55% marks - from AICTE/UGC approved University. Mandatory: FRM (GARP USA) / CFA (CFA Institute USA) / PRM (PRMIA USA). Preferable: Certification in SPSS/SAS, R, Python. Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management/Compliance Dept. in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.
AGM - Asset Liability Management (Scale V) Full-time BE/B.Tech OR Post Graduate Degree/Diploma in Business Management/Banking/Finance OR Chartered Accountant. Mandatory: FRM (GARP) / PRM (PRMIA USA) / CFA (CFA Institute). Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager. Desirable: ALM/Interest Rate Risk/Liquidity Risk management.
AGM - Digital Risk & Analytics (Scale V) Full-time Bachelor's in Mathematics/Statistics OR B.E./B.Tech in Data Science/IT/CS/Software Engineering. Preferred: MBA/PGDM/PGDBM/PGDBA in Finance OR PG in Statistics/Mathematics/Economics/Econometrics. Preferred: FRM by GARP, RAI by GARP, PRM (PRMIA), CFA, CQF, any Certification in ML/AI/NLP/Web Crawling/Neural Networks. Minimum 10 years in Banking (Officer Cadre), out of which minimum 5 years in Risk Management in scheduled commercial banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager. Desirable: Digital Risk/Vendor Risk/IT Risk/Model Validation.
AGM - Finance & Accounts (Scale V) Mandatory: Full-time Graduation AND must have passed final exam conducted by ICAI and enrolled as member of ICAI (Chartered Accountant). CA membership mandatory. Minimum 10 years in Scheduled Commercial Bank having more than 2000 branches, out of which minimum 7 years at Bank's Head Office/Administrative Office handling Corporate Taxation, GST, TDS, Balance Sheet, IFRS, Ind AS, Regulatory Reporting, Capital Raising. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.
AGM - Credit Officer (Scale V) Chartered Accountant / CFA / CMA-ICWA from recognized institute OR Two Years Full-time Post Graduation in Banking/Finance/any credit related field from recognized university/institution/board. As per qualification above. Minimum 10 years post qualification experience as an officer, out of which 7 years in Corporate Credit/Project Finance/Mid & Large Credit in Scheduled Public/Private Sector Banks having more than 2000 branches. Current role must be Chief Manager with min. 1 year OR min. 4 years as Senior Manager.

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit & Relaxation

Age limit for all posts is Minimum 33 years and Maximum 45 years as on 31-03-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines as follows:

Post Name Age Limit
AGM - Risk Management (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years
AGM - Compliance / Risk Management (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years
AGM - Asset Liability Management (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years
AGM - Digital Risk & Analytics (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years
AGM - Finance & Accounts (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years
AGM - Credit Officer (Scale V) Min 33 years - Max 45 years

 

Category Age Relaxation
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5 years
Other Backward Classes (OBC) 3 years
PwBD (Divyangjan) 10 years (15 years for SC/ST PwBD; 13 years for OBC PwBD) - subject to maximum age of 56 years
Children/Family members of those who died in 1984 riots 5 years
Ex-Servicemen (with at least 5 years military service) 5 years

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fees / intimation charges are payable online from 30-04-2026 to 25-05-2026. The fee once paid is non-refundable. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

Category Amount (inclusive of GST)
SC / ST / PwBD (Divyangjan) / Women Candidates Rs. 175/- (Intimation Charges only)
All Other Candidates (General / OBC / EWS) Rs. 850/- (Application Fee + Intimation Charges)

Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process consists of an Online Examination followed by an Interview. Merely satisfying the eligibility norm does not entitle a candidate to be called for the test or interview. Candidates qualified in the online test will be called for interview to the extent of 4 times the number of vacancies. Final merit will be based on the combined performance in Online Test and Interview in the ratio of 70:30. In case of equal marks, candidates senior in age will be placed higher in the merit list.

Online Examination Structure:

Sr. No. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
1 Stream/Category Specific Questions 70 70 Composite time of 60 minutes
2 Banking, Present Economic Scenario & General Awareness 30 30
Total 100  

Qualifying Marks

Category Qualifying Marks - Written Test Qualifying Marks - Interview
General / EWS 50% 50%
SC / ST / OBC / PwBD (Divyangjan) 45% 45%

Selection Weightage

Online Test : Interview Weightage 70 : 30
No. of candidates called for Interview 4 times the number of vacancies (category-wise)
Negative Marking No penalty for wrong answers

How to Apply for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026

Candidates can apply only online from 30-04-2026 to 07-06-2026 at the link https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/cbiagmapr26/. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates should scan their photograph (200x230 pixels, 20-50 KB), signature (140x60 pixels, 10-20 KB), left thumb impression (240x240 pixels, 20-50 KB), and handwritten declaration (800x400 pixels, 50-100 KB). A valid personal email ID and contact number must be kept active till completion of the recruitment process. Not more than one application should be submitted by any candidate. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

An indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed by the selected candidate to indemnify the Bank in case such candidate leaves the service within 3 years of selection. Candidates must have a minimum CIC (Credit Information Company) score of 650 with no credit default or financial indiscipline as on the date of document verification.

Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview (Original + Self-attested copies):

  • Original Marks Sheet & Provisional Certificate / Degree Certificate issued from University
  • Experience Certificates as per eligibility requirements
  • Caste / PwBD (Divyangjan) / Caste Validity Certificate (wherever applicable)
  • OBC Certificate with Non-Creamy Layer clause (for OBC candidates)
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates)
  • No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Govt./PSU/Nationalized Banks)
  • Colour passport size photograph (same as uploaded in application form)

Apply Online at: https://ibpsreg.ibps.in/cbiagmapr26/

Important Instructions for Central Bank of India AGM Specialist Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only one application per candidate - multiple applications will result in forfeiture of fee paid for other registrations.
  • Applications once submitted cannot be withdrawn and fee once paid is neither refunded nor held in reserve.
  • Candidates must ensure eligibility before applying - the Bank reserves the right to reject applications at any stage.
  • Minimum CIC score of 650 is mandatory with no credit default in the credit report.
  • Indemnity bond of INR 3,00,000/- must be executed if selected (valid for 3 years of service).
  • Probation period of 2 years from the date of joining.
  • Initial posting will be at Mumbai and thereafter transferable anywhere in India as per bank's transfer policy.
  • Government/PSU/Nationalized Bank employees must produce NOC from employer at the time of interview.
  • OBC candidates in 'Creamy Layer' will be treated as 'General Category'.
  • No Travelling Allowance is payable to candidates called for interview. However, unemployed SC/ST candidates will be paid 2nd Class Rail/Ordinary Bus fare on production of travel evidence.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Any resulting dispute shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of Courts situated at Mumbai.
  • Candidates are advised to regularly visit the Bank's website www.centralbank.bank.in for updates under Career section.
  • All decisions of the Bank regarding eligibility, selection, and other matters are final and binding on all candidates.

Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology / Pharmacy / ECG Technology / General Nursing & Midwifery / X-Ray Technology / Ophthalmic Technology Diploma in respective Paramedical discipline + Valid registration certificate of respective state Nil

NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must satisfy all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date, i.e. 01-06-2026. Candidature is provisional and subject to verification of original documents before joining.

Criteria Details
Nationality Indian Nationals only.
Institute Location Qualification must be from a recognized Institute/University which is functional and operational either in Uttar Pradesh or Madhya Pradesh State.
Educational Qualification As prescribed discipline-wise; obtained through Full-time Regular course only. Qualifications via Part-time/Correspondence/Distance Learning are not eligible.
Qualification Passing Date Candidate should not have passed Graduation/Diploma/ITI prior to June 2022.
Prior Apprenticeship / Experience Candidates who have undergone Apprenticeship earlier, are currently pursuing Apprenticeship Training, or have 1 year or more of job experience are summarily ineligible.
Higher Qualification Candidates possessing higher qualification over and above the prescribed qualification are not eligible.
OBC Status Only OBC Non-Creamy Layer candidates are eligible for reservation benefits.
PwBD Eligibility PwBD candidates must submit a valid disability certificate as per Rights of Persons with Disabilities Rules 2017 from the competent authority.

NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 Age Limit

Age is calculated as on the cut-off date 01-06-2026. Candidates must have been born on or between 01-06-2000 and 01-06-2008 (i.e. minimum 18 years and maximum 26 years). Standard age relaxations are applicable only for reserved category slots.

Category Age Relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
PwBD (General/UR) 10 years relaxation
PwBD (SC/ST) 15 years relaxation
PwBD (OBC Non-Creamy Layer) 13 years relaxation

NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 Application Fee

There is no application fee mentioned in the notification. Candidates apply online through the NATS/NAPS government portals. No paper-based application will be accepted.

Category Application Fee
All Categories NIL (Apply through NATS/NAPS portal)

NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 Selection Process

Selection will be made purely on the basis of merit drawn from qualifying examination marks, followed by document scrutiny and a mandatory medical test. No written examination will be conducted by NCL for this recruitment.

Stage 1 – Merit List Preparation

For Graduate and Diploma positions, merit is drawn on the basis of marks scored in the qualifying Graduation/Diploma examination. For ITI Trade positions, merit is based on the average of passing percentage in Matriculation and the respective National/State Trade Certificate. Candidates are arranged in descending order of marks. In case of a tie, date of birth (older candidate preferred) and then alphabetical order of name will be applied.

Stage 2 – Document Scrutiny

Shortlisted candidates based on merit will be called to appear with all original certificates for scrutiny. Date and venue will be notified on nclcil.in and through NATS/NAPS portal or email. Failure to appear or produce valid documents within the stipulated date and time will result in cancellation of candidature.

Stage 3 – Medical Examination

Final offer for Apprenticeship Training is subject to clearing the Medical Test as per prescribed standards under the Mines Rules 1955. A fitness certificate from the medical officer of a Government Hospital (rank not less than Assistant Civil Surgeon) in the prescribed format (Annexure-3) must be produced. The Medical Fitness certificate must be issued after the date of release of this Notification.

Who should NOT apply for NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27?

  • Candidates who have already undergone Apprenticeship Training earlier or are currently pursuing Apprenticeship in any industry.
  • Candidates with 1 year or more of job experience.
  • Candidates who have passed their qualifying degree/diploma/ITI before June 2022.
  • Candidates who possess qualifications higher than the prescribed qualification for the applied discipline.
  • Candidates whose qualifying institution is not functional and operational in Uttar Pradesh or Madhya Pradesh.
  • Candidates whose qualifications were obtained through Part-time, Correspondence, or Distance Learning mode.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the Creamy Layer.
  • Candidates who submit multiple applications - multiple applications will lead to disqualification.
  • Candidates who are unable to produce a valid Medical Fitness certificate as per Mines Rules 1955.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27?

  • Not pre-registering on the NATS portal (for Graduate/Diploma) or NAPS portal (for Trade/ITI) before applying - without a valid Enrolment ID, the application cannot be completed.
  • Applying for more than one discipline - multiple applications will lead to disqualification of candidature.
  • Entering incorrect percentage/marks scored or maximum marks in the online form - candidature will be cancelled if found incorrect during scrutiny.
  • Not checking that the qualifying institution is functional and operational in Uttar Pradesh or Madhya Pradesh.
  • Uploading blurred or unclear photograph or signature in black ink.
  • Not having an active email ID and mobile number valid for at least one year for future communication.
  • Rounding off the passing percentage - NCL will not consider rounding off (49.99% will be treated as less than 50%).
  • Not taking a printout of the finally submitted online application for future reference.

How to Apply for NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27

Candidates must apply online only. Graduate and Diploma Apprentice candidates must first enrol on the NATS portal at https://nats.education.gov.in and Trade Apprentice candidates must enrol on the NAPS portal at https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. After successful registration, a registration number (Enrolment ID) will be generated, following which the candidate must apply for the NCL positions through the respective portal.

Documents/Details required at the time of Online Application:

  • Valid NATS/NAPS Enrolment ID (registration reference).
  • Scanned colour photograph and scanned signature in black ink (as per portal specifications).
  • Date of Birth as per Matriculation certificate.
  • Academic details - total marks scored and maximum marks in qualifying degree/diploma/ITI trade.
  • Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) in prescribed format (Annexure-1), if applicable.
  • PwBD disability certificate as per Rights of Persons with Disabilities Rules 2017 (Annexure-2), if applicable.
  • Address for correspondence and permanent address.

Candidates must save and preserve a hard copy as well as soft copy of the finally submitted online application for future reference. No paper-based or postal applications will be accepted.

Apply Online (Graduate/Diploma) at: https://nats.education.gov.in

Apply Online (Trade/ITI) at: https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in

For assistance, candidates may email the HRD Department, NCL at [email protected] or visit Menu > Career > Apprenticeship Training section on the NCL official website www.nclcil.in.

Important Instructions for NCL Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026-27 Candidates

  • Online applications are open from 01-06-2026 to 15-06-2026 through NATS/NAPS portals only.
  • A candidate may apply for any one notified position only - multiple applications will lead to disqualification.
  • Information pertaining to Date of Birth, Caste Category, and Qualification once entered in the online application shall be considered final - no alteration will be permitted at later stages.
  • Stipend for Graduate and Diploma Apprentices is inclusive of the Government of India share paid through DBT; Trade Apprentice stipend will be paid directly by NCL.
  • Stipend will be paid directly into the AADHAAR-linked bank account of the Apprenticeship Trainee as per the signed contract.
  • Apprenticeship Training is purely temporary and statutory in nature - it does not confer any right or claim for regular employment in NCL.
  • Apprentices are eligible for 11 days Casual Leave and 15 days Medical Leave per year of training.
  • Engagement hours are between 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM.
  • Apprentices will be covered under the Workmen Compensation Act, 1923 for any accident during training.
  • No accommodation, fooding/lodging, or transport will be provided during the training period.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in document scrutiny or post-scrutiny processes.
  • Canvassing in any manner at any stage will lead to cancellation of candidature.
  • Any dispute will be subject to courts/tribunals of Singrauli, MP only.
  • Dates mentioned in the notification are tentative and subject to alteration/amendment.

How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About this page: Sarkari Job 2026 information on this page is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team from official government recruitment notifications. All vacancy data is verified against official websites before listing. OnlyJobs has been India's trusted government job portal since 2011, serving 10+ million users annually. For any recruitment, always refer to the official notification linked above.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering
  • Category-wise Jobs: General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, PwD

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 987+ active job listings.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.

  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is OnlyJobs?

OnlyJobs is a trusted Indian job and exam information portal. Find latest sarkari job vacancies, exam results, admit cards, online forms at OnlyJobs.OnlyJobs.

Q2: How can I check the latest Sarkari jobs at OnlyJobs?

You can check the latest Sarkari jobs on OnlyJobs by visiting the latest jobs page, scanning the newest recruitment listings, and clicking the job title to see full details such as eligibility, application date, and official notification.

Q3: Does OnlyJobs provide सरकारी नौकरी updates?

Yes, OnlyJobs shares daily updates on सरकारी नौकरी, including vacancies from Railway, SSC, Banking, UPSC, Police, Army, and state-level recruitments.

Q4: Can I find admit cards and answer keys on OnlyJobs?

Yes, the website also publishes admit cards, answer keys, syllabus, cut-off marks, and other important exam-related notifications.Result notification service.

Q5: Is OnlyJobs an official government website?

No, OnlyJobs is a private information portal. It collects and publishes recruitment and exam updates from official government sources for public convenience.

Q6: Is this the official OnlyJobs website?

No. OnlyJobs is a private information portal, not a government website. All results, admit cards, and notifications link to their respective official government portals such as ssc.nic.in, upsc.gov.in, rrbcdg.gov.in, and ibps.in. OnlyJobs has been providing this aggregation service since 2011.

Q7: How do I get OnlyJobs alerts on my phone?

Download the OnlyJobs mobile app from Store, join the official WhatsApp Channel, or subscribe to the Telegram group. You will receive instant push notifications whenever a new result, admit card, or recruitment notification is published.

Q8: How can I check my OnlyJobs 2026?

Visit OnlyJobs.OnlyJobs daily. Find your exam in the Latest Results section, click the link, and you will be taken to the official government portal. Enter your Roll Number or Registration ID to view your result.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

CSC e-Governance Services India Ltd (CSC) has published the Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Notification 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill multiple Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator positions across various districts in Telangana, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, and Odisha. Candidates aged 18 years and above who possess 12th Pass/Intermediate, 10th Pass with 2 years ITI, or 10th Pass with 3 years Polytechnic Diploma along with a valid UIDAI Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate are eligible to apply online through the official CSC portal. The application process is active and will remain open until 20 June 2026. Candidates can easily access the notification details and apply online through the links provided below.

FOLLOW US

Particulars Details
Organization CSC e-Governance Services India Ltd
Name of Post Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator - District
Work Location Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) in various districts
Engagement Type Purely Contractual
Contract Period 1 Year
Minimum Age 18 years and above
Last Date to Apply 20 June 2026
Job Type Semi-skilled Aadhaar enrolment/updation work

Eligibility Criteria for Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator

Age Limit

  • Minimum age: 18 years as on the last date of application.
  • Upper age limit, if any, will be as per CSC/concerned guidelines.

Educational Qualification

Candidate must have any one of the following qualifications:

  • 12th / Intermediate / Senior Secondary, or
  • Matriculation / 10th with 2 years ITI, or
  • Matriculation / 10th with 3 years Polytechnic Diploma.

Aadhaar Certification

  • Candidate should possess a valid Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate issued by a Testing & Certifying Agency authorized by UIDAI.
  • The certificate must be valid at the time of application and during the contract period.

Other Skills

  • Candidate should have basic computer skills.
  • Familiarity with Aadhaar enrolment/update software and biometric devices will be preferred.

Remuneration

Remuneration will be as per the minimum wages of the respective State prescribed for semi-skilled manpower. Final payout structure and other benefits, if any, will be as per CSC e-Governance Services India Ltd norms.

Aadhaar Supervisor Exam & LMS Certification Details

Aadhaar Supervisor Exam

Candidates can apply for and take the Aadhaar Supervisor Exam through the official exam portal: Aadhaar Supervisor Exam.

After submitting the exam application, an authorization request goes to the concerned State Team. Once the State Team approves the request, the candidate becomes eligible to appear in the Aadhaar Supervisor Exam.

Aadhaar LMS Certification

LMS ID and password will be created and approved for eligible candidates. After approval, candidates can take the Aadhaar LMS Certification through the UIDAI e-learning portal: Aadhaar LMS Certification.

Other Terms and Conditions

  • The engagement is purely on contract basis for a period of 1 year only.
  • CSC e-Governance Services India Ltd reserves the right to cancel or modify the recruitment process at any stage.
  • Only candidates with valid Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor certificates are eligible.
  • VLEs (Village Level Entrepreneurs) are not eligible to apply for these positions.

How to Apply for CSC Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator Recruitment 2026

  • Interested and eligible candidates should check their eligibility including age, qualification and Aadhaar certification before applying.
  • Visit the CSC ASK information page for details and updates: CSC Aadhaar Seva Kendra ASK.
  • Follow the application instructions issued by CSC e-Governance Services India Ltd for the respective state/district.
  • Keep all required documents and Aadhaar certification details ready while applying.
  • The last date for submission of application is 20 June 2026.

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL), a Government of India Enterprise, has released an official advertisement for 100 posts of Junior Telecom Officer – Direct Recruitment (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR] from the open market.

FOLLOW US

This recruitment is under Advt. No. BSNLCO-11/12(11)/1/2026-RECTT-CO dated May 26, 2026. Both internal and external candidates fulfilling the prescribed eligibility conditions are eligible to apply. The deadline for online registration is July 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM.

BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Key Dates

Important Dates Details

Online Registration End Date July 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM

Essential administrative details regarding the vacancy package include:

Parameter Details
Post Name Junior Telecom Officer – Direct Recruitment (Telecom) [JTO(T)-DR]
Total Vacancies 100
Pay Scale IDA Pay Scale [E-1] of ₹16,400 – ₹40,500/- (2nd PRC pay scale w.e.f. 01.01.2007)
Probation Period Two Years
Bond ₹6 Lakhs bond to serve BSNL for a minimum of 5 years from date of appointment

Eligibility Criteria & Job Requirements

Candidates must fulfill the minimum qualifications specified for the Telecom stream:

Educational Qualification

  • Core Qualification (Telecom Stream): Bachelor of Engineering Degree or equivalent from a Central Govt./State Govt. recognized Institution/University in any of the following disciplines: Telecommunications, Electronics, Radio, Computer, Electrical, Information Technology (IT), or Instrumentation.
  • Alternative Qualification: M.Sc. (Electronics) or M.Sc. (Computer Science).
  • Higher Qualifications: Candidates possessing M.Tech. in the prescribed disciplines are also eligible to appear in the Direct Recruitment examination.
  • Vision Requirement: The applicant must be capable of distinguishing principal colours and should have binocular vision.
  • Nationality: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply.

Age Limit & Category-wise Relaxation

General Age Limit: Minimum 20 years and maximum 30 years as on the closing date of receipt of application.

Category Maximum Age Limit

BSNL Employees – OBC (NCL) 38 Years (30+8)
BSNL Employees – SC / ST 40 Years (30+10)
BSNL Employees (PwBD) – OBC (NCL) 48 Years (30+18)
BSNL Employees (PwBD) – SC / ST 50 Years (30+20)

Application Fee

Category Examination Fee
UR / OBC / EWS Candidates ₹2,000/-
SC / ST / PwBD Candidates ₹1,000/-

Fee must be paid through online mode only (Internet Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card) on the BSNL website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in. Fee once paid is non-refundable.

BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026 Selection Methodology

The selection process is conducted through a Computer Based Multiple Choice Objective Type Test (CBT) as follows:

  1. Written Examination (CBT): 3 hours duration, comprising 120 questions for a total of 480 marks across three sections.
Section Particulars Questions Maximum Marks
Section I Engineering Stream Section – I 50 Questions 200 Marks
Section II Engineering Stream Section – II 50 Questions 200 Marks
Section III General Ability Test Section – III 20 Questions 80 Marks
Total 120 Questions 480 Marks
  1. Marking Scheme: +4 marks for each correct answer and -1 mark for each wrong answer (negative marking applicable).
  2. Minimum Qualifying Marks: Minimum 40% in each section individually. Overall minimum qualifying marks in all three sections combined: 50% for Unreserved/OBC (CL) candidates; 45% for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/PwBD/EWS candidates.
  3. Document Verification: Selected candidates in the declared merit list will be called for document verification. Date and venue will be intimated in due course.
  4. Medical Examination: Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to passing BSNL's Medical Examination as per standards laid down by BSNL.

How to Apply for BSNL JTO Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must complete the online registration process as follows:

  1. Visit the BSNL official examination website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in and register online only. No manual/paper applications will be entertained.
  2. Keep the following ready before registering: valid mobile number and email ID (for OTP), scanned passport size colour photograph (jpg/jpeg, 20–50 kb, white background), scanned signature (jpg/jpeg, 10–20 kb), Class 10th Certificate (date of birth proof), Degree Certificate and consolidated marksheet, photo identity proof (Aadhaar/Passport/Driving License/Voter ID), valid category/reservation certificate as applicable, and Medical Certificate for PwBD candidates.
  3. Pay the requisite non-refundable examination fee online through Internet Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card.
  4. After completing registration, take a printout of the computer-generated registration form and retain it for future reference.
  5. An editing window will be open for 7 days after the registration period closes (July 4–11, 2026). Selective fields can be edited during this period only.
  6. Candidates currently employed in Government Departments/Public Sector Undertakings/Autonomous Bodies must produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their present employer at the time of document verification.
  7. Candidates must select up to 21 Circle preferences (in order of preference) from the registration portal dropdown menu. Options once exercised shall be final.
  8. Admit cards can be downloaded from the BSNL website using the registered username and password well before the examination date.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, NBCC Building Block 3, Kidwai Nagar, New Delhi – 110023 has officially released a notification dated 21/05/2026 for engagement of 635 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official Punjab & Sind Bank website.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Punjab & Sind Bank is recruiting 635 Apprentice posts with Regular Graduation qualification, last date 06 June 2026.

Organization Name Punjab & Sind Bank (A Govt. of India Undertaking), H.O. HRD Department, New Delhi
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Human Resource Development (HRD) Department, Punjab & Sind Bank
Total Training Seats 635 (across 29 States/UTs, including PwBD seats)

Duration of Training 12 Months (On-the-Job Training; 10:00 AM to 05:00 PM)
Application Mode Online only (via NAPS portal + NATS portal + PSB website)
Selection Mode Merit-based on HSC / 10+2 marks (State-wise, District-wise, Category-wise) + Medical Examination
Mandatory Requirement Prior registration with 100% completed profile on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/)

A total of 635 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the respective cut-off dates. Only candidates with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals are eligible to apply through the Bank's website. Candidates can apply for only one State and one District and must not submit more than one application.

Criteria Details
Nationality / Citizenship Citizen of India / Subject of Nepal or Bhutan / Tibetan Refugee (came before 01-01-1962) / Person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries - with eligibility certificate from Govt. of India where applicable.
Portal Registration (Mandatory) 100% completed profile on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/) before applying on PSB website.
Educational Qualification (as on 06-06-2026) Regular Graduation in any discipline from a recognized University/Institute or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government.
Post Graduate Candidates Candidates who have completed Post Graduate qualification are NOT eligible.
Work Experience Candidates with work experience of 1 year or more after educational qualification are NOT eligible.
Years Since Qualifying Exam Candidates should not have completed 5 years after passing the qualifying examination.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing Apprenticeship in any organisation under the Apprentices Act 1961 are NOT eligible.
Local Language Proficiency The candidate must be proficient in the local language (Reading, Writing, Speaking & Understanding) of the applied State/UT and must produce 8th/10th/12th or Graduate level certificate evidencing having studied the local language.
Aadhaar Requirement Every apprentice must have a valid Aadhaar number before engagement / contract signing.

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-05-2026. Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
SC / ST / PwBD Rs. 100/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges
General / EWS / OBC Rs. 200/- + Applicable Taxes + Payment Gateway charges

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be merit-based on HSC/10+2 marks. No written examination is conducted. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, district-wise vacancy availability, and medical fitness. In case of a tie in marks, candidates will be ranked by age in descending order within their respective category, state, and district.

Stage 1 - Merit List (Based on HSC / 10+2 Marks)

Candidates who fulfill minimum eligibility criteria will be placed in a merit list in descending order of their 10+2 marks, drawn State-wise, District-wise, and Category-wise. No written examination is conducted for this recruitment. Candidates must select their preferred State and District in the application itself. In case of equal marks, tie-break is by age in descending order within the respective category, state, and district.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Shortlisted candidates will be intimated through email only and must appear for document verification at their own expense. Original documents along with self-attested photocopies must be produced. Candidates failing to produce originals will not be allowed to proceed further.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank.

Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - District-wise Vacancy Details

Candidates must apply for one specific district within their chosen State. District-wise allotment will be subject to vacancy availability and administrative requirements of the Bank.

Competition Level Analysis for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for PSB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be moderate to high. With only 635 seats spread across 29 States/UTs, the vacancy count is significantly lower than comparable bank apprenticeship drives such as SBI. Selection is purely merit-based on 10+2 marks without any written examination, which means candidates with high HSC scores - especially from states like Uttar Pradesh (100 seats), Punjab (90 seats), Bihar (40 seats), Maharashtra (30 seats), and Haryana (50 seats) - will face intense competition within their district and category.

Since there is no written exam, the effective competition depends entirely on the candidate's 10+2 percentage and age as a tie-breaker. Attrition at later stages will occur through local language proof verification, document verification, and medical fitness, which will reduce the final eligible pool. Candidates who do not have proof of studying the local language at 8th/10th/12th level are at a disadvantage as they must demonstrate local language proficiency separately.

Who should NOT apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job; this is a 12-month apprenticeship only, with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment in the Bank.
  • Candidates who have already completed any Apprenticeship (in PSB or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates who have completed Post Graduate (PG) qualification - PG holders are explicitly not eligible.
  • Candidates with 1 year or more of work/job experience after completion of educational qualification.
  • Candidates who have completed more than 5 years after passing their qualifying examination.
  • Candidates outside the age band of 20–28 years as on 01-05-2026, except those eligible for notified relaxation under SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/widow/1984 riot victim categories.
  • Aspirants who do not hold a Regular Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute by 06-06-2026.
  • Candidates not proficient in the local language of the State/UT they are applying for, and who cannot produce 8th/10th/12th or graduation-level certificate evidencing study of that language.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register with 100% completed profile on both NAPS (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and NATS (nats.education.gov.in) portals before applying - this is a mandatory prerequisite.
  • Candidates without a valid Aadhaar number, as it is mandatory before engagement/contract signing.
  • Candidates applying with a Ration Card or Learner's Driving License as ID proof - these are not accepted as valid identity documents for this process.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Not completing 100% profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying; incomplete portal profiles will render the candidate ineligible.
  • Applying for a wrong State or District where the candidate is not proficient in the local language and cannot produce language study certificates.
  • Entering the wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed after submission and any mismatch at document verification can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from the creamy layer applying under OBC reservation instead of UR; only Non-Creamy Layer OBC candidates with a valid, recent certificate are entitled to reservation.
  • Uploading blurred or incorrect photograph, signature, thumb impression or handwritten declaration (wrong size, format, or written by someone else) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using an invalid or inactive mobile number or email ID, resulting in failure to receive OTPs, shortlisting intimations, or further communication from the Bank.
  • Submitting multiple applications; only the last valid completed application will be retained and fees paid for other registrations will be forfeited.
  • Signing the application in CAPITAL LETTERS; signatures in capital letters are not acceptable and must match across all documents and correspondence.
  • Forgetting to note down the Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal, which is mandatory to be mentioned while applying for training seats.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later disputing about permanent absorption after the 12-month apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online through the Bank's website (https://punjabandsind.bank.in/) only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must mandatorily complete 100% registration on both the NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in/) and the NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in/). Candidates must also note down their Enrollment ID generated on the NATS portal as it is required while applying.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (to be signed across by the candidate on printing)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be accepted)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink - text: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at time of online application)
  • Image files must be in .jpg / .jpeg format; scanned documents must be in PDF format only

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Joining:

  • Xth / Date of Birth certificate in support of Date of Birth
  • Printout of application form
  • All certificates and mark sheets of educational qualification from Standard Xth, XII, and Graduation onwards
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format for SC/ST candidates
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed format, valid as on date of joining (issued within one year prior to joining); OBC Caste Name must tally with Central Government list
  • Income and Asset Certificate in prescribed format issued by competent authority, valid EWS certificate for the financial year, for EWS category candidates
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board for PwBD candidates
  • Age relaxation proof with valid documents (e.g., District Magistrate certificate for 1984 riot-affected persons)
  • Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card / Passport / Permanent Driving License / Voter’s Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Aadhaar / E-Aadhaar / Identity Card issued by recognised College/University / Gazetted Officer certificate) - bearing the same name as on the application / call letter. Ration Card and Learner’s Driving License are NOT valid.
  • Two passport-size photographs similar to the one used during portal registration
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

NO DOCUMENT SHOULD BE DIRECTLY SENT TO THE BANK BEFORE OR AFTER THE PROCESS. Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to the Bank. Candidates must appear for document verification at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://punjabandsind.bank.in/ → Home Page → Recruitment → Relevant Notification

For queries: Email [email protected] | All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only.

Important Instructions for Punjab & Sind Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of the Bank and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for Bank employees.
  • The Bank shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to any Apprentice who has completed the period of training, nor shall the Apprentice be obligated to accept employment. The Apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training.
  • Candidates can apply for only one State and one District, and not more than one application should be submitted. In case of multiple applications, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained and fees for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • Mandatory registration with 100% completed profile on both NAPS and NATS portals before applying on the Bank's website is required.
  • Candidates who had training or job experience of one or more years after completion of educational qualifications shall not be eligible for engagement as apprentice.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General/UR.
  • Change of address / email ID / phone number / caste / category will not be entertained after submission of the application.
  • Signatures in CAPITAL LETTERS will not be acceptable. The signature must be consistent across all documents and correspondence.
  • No daily allowance, conveyance allowance or travelling allowance will be paid to Apprentices. No overtime is permitted. Apprentices are entitled to only 1 casual leave per month (maximum 4 at a time).
  • The Contract of Apprenticeship shall terminate automatically on expiry of the 12-month training period. A non-performing apprentice will be given 7 days' notice. An apprentice wishing to terminate early must give 15 days' prior notice to the Bank.
  • Apprentices must devote their entire time exclusively to the Bank and shall not engage in any other commercial business, service, or advisory activity during the engagement period.
  • Apprentices must not disclose any financial information, data, concepts or internal communications relating to the Bank's business to any unauthorized person.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Any resultant dispute arising out of this advertisement shall be subject to the sole jurisdiction of the Courts situated at Delhi.
  • Bank reserves the right to change/add/cancel the selection process or cancel the entire process at any stage without notice or refund of application fees.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only - candidates should visit regularly.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest State Bank of India (SBI) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Apprentice posts! Published on 19-05-2026 at the official website of SBI, this new recruitment drive offers 7150 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 20 to 28 years age group and possessing a Graduation degree from a recognized University/Institute can apply online from 19-05-2026 to 08-06-2026. Find all details about the SBI Apprentice new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

State Bank of India, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CRPD/APPR/2026-27/07 dated 19/05/2026 for engagement of 7150 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise and district-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification before applying. The notification is available on the official SBI website.

  • Download SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

State Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

SBI is recruiting 7150 Apprentice posts with Any Graduate qualification, last date 08 June 2026.

Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)
Department Central Recruitment & Promotion Department (CRPD), State Bank of India

Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)

Selection Mode Online Written Test + Test of Local Language + Medical Examination

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 7150 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the date of eligibility (01-04-2026). Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates must mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online. Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online through SBI website (https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers).
Educational Qualification (as on 01-04-2026) Graduation from a recognized University / Institute.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship either in SBI or in any other organization earlier, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training as per the Apprentices Act 1961 as amended.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after attainment of educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
CIBIL Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid outstanding till date of issuance of offer of engagement shall not be eligible.
Aadhaar Requirement All candidates (except those belonging to Northeastern States where alternate ID proof will do) should produce Aadhaar Number before engagement, i.e., contract signing for apprenticeship training.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years as on 01-04-2026, i.e., candidates must have been born not later than 01-04-2006 and not earlier than 02-04-1998 (both days inclusive). Maximum age indicated is for Unreserved and EWS candidates. Relaxation in upper age limit is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates.

General (UR) / EWS 20 to 28 years (as on 01-04-2026)
SC / ST 5 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (as per Govt. of India guidelines)
PwBD As per Government of India guidelines

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Fees/Intimation Charges through online mode only. The process of registration is complete only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date for payment of fees. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be held in reserve for any other exam or selection.

Category Fees / Intimation Charges
General / OBC / EWS Rs. 300/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD NIL

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on (i) Online Written Test and (ii) Test of Local Language, followed by Medical Examination. Final selection is subject to verification of eligibility, qualifying in local language test, and medical fitness. A Wait List (State-category wise) will be maintained for one year from the date of result or next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier. Candidates will be released only once from this waitlist against non-joining subject to availability of qualified candidates.

Stage 1 - Online Written Examination (Objective Type)

Sl. Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test

2 General English 25 25 15 minutes English only
3 Quantitative Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
4 Reasoning Ability & Computer Aptitude 25 25 15 minutes As per State (see table below)
Total 100 100 60 minutes  

Each test has a separate timing as mentioned above. There will be NEGATIVE MARKS for wrong answers — 1/4th of the mark assigned for the question will be deducted for each wrong answer. Test questions (except General English) will be set in 13 Regional Languages viz. Assamese, Bengali, Gujarati, Kannada, Konkani, Malayalam, Manipuri, Marathi, Odia, Punjabi, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu — in addition to English and Hindi — as applicable State-wise. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order; tie-break is by age in descending order.
