Stage 2 - Test of Local Language

The test of specified local language will be conducted after qualifying the online written examination. Candidates who fail to qualify this test will not be engaged as apprentice. Candidates who produce 10th or 12th standard mark sheet/certificate evidencing having studied the specified local language will not be required to undergo the language test. Candidates must appear at their own expense; SBI will not reimburse travelling expenses.

Stage 3 - Medical Examination

Engagement of selected apprentices is subject to being declared medically fit as per the requirement of the Bank and satisfactory completion of KYE formalities.

SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Weightage in Junior Associate Recruitment

Weightage/Relaxation to apprentices in recruitment of Junior Associates shall be given as per the recruitment policy of the Bank as amended from time to time, to apprentices who successfully complete the apprenticeship training in SBI, subject to candidate's satisfactory conduct and performance during the entire training period. Successful completion refers to passing of the skill evaluation test (Trade test) and obtention of the joint National Apprenticeship Certificate issued by SBI-NSDC / BFSI-SSC.

Competition Level Analysis for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The competition level for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is expected to be very high due to 7150 seats being offered at the all-India level and the exam being open to any graduate, including those already preparing for bank and SSC exams. While the written test is of easy to moderate difficulty and slightly below SBI Clerk level, the large number of serious aspirants in high-population states like Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, West Bengal, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana will push state-wise and category-wise cut-offs upward.

In practical terms, each seat is likely to attract dozens to over a hundred candidates in major states, making speed, accuracy, and sectional balance crucial to clearing the single-stage written exam followed by the local language test. However, the effective competition reduces at later stages because some candidates will fail to qualify the language test or be filtered out at the document verification, CIBIL, Aadhaar, and medical fitness stages, and SBI will finally select candidates strictly on state-wise and category-wise merit from those who clear all stages.

Who should NOT apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates looking for a permanent job in SBI; this is only a 1‑year apprenticeship with no guarantee of absorption or regular employment.
  • Candidates who already completed any Apprenticeship (in SBI or any other organisation) or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act.
  • Candidates outside the age band 20–28 years as on the cut-off date (even 1 day younger/older is not eligible, except notified relaxations for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD).
  • Aspirants who do not have a Graduation degree from a recognised University/Institute by the specified date.
  • Candidates not fluent in the notified local language of the State/UT (reading, writing, speaking, understanding), and who are unlikely to clear the language test or do not have 10th/12th proof.
  • Candidates unwilling or unable to register on the NAPS portal before applying, as this is a mandatory prerequisite for SBI Apprentice.
  • Candidates with serious CIBIL/loan/credit‑card defaults who have not regularised or repaid outstanding dues by the time of offer; such profiles are normally rejected in bank recruitments.
  • Ex‑Servicemen / Disabled Ex‑Servicemen, where the notification clearly states no engagement / no reservation for them under Apprentice.
  • PwBD candidates whose disability type falls under “likely to improve” (temporary disability) where reservation benefits may not apply as per notification conditions.
  • Candidates who cannot produce valid Aadhaar (except where exempted, e.g., certain North‑Eastern states with alternate ID allowed).

Common mistakes / What to check before applying SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026?

  • Applying in a wrong State or language where you cannot comfortably read, write and speak the specified local language.
  • Entering wrong category (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR); category cannot be changed later and mismatch with certificates at DV can lead to cancellation.
  • OBC candidates from creamy layer applying as OBC instead of UR; only non‑creamy OBCs with valid, recent certificate get reservation.
  • Uploading blurred/incorrect photo, signature, thumb impression or declaration (wrong size, wrong format, someone else’s handwriting) leading to rejection at scrutiny.
  • Using invalid or inactive mobile number/email ID, causing failure to receive OTPs, registration details, call letters or further communication.
  • Submitting multiple applications for different states or with different details; SBI usually accepts only the last complete application and may forfeit extra fees.
  • Ignoring negative marking and over‑attempting in the exam, which can sharply reduce your score despite high attempts.
  • Not reading the clause that this is not regular employment, and later protesting about absorption or permanent posting after apprenticeship ends.

How to Apply for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Candidates shall apply online; no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register on the NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and read the detailed guidelines on https://bfsissc.com before applying online through the SBI website.

Candidates should have a valid email ID and mobile number which should be kept active till declaration of results. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till the last date.

Documents Required to be Scanned and Uploaded (as per Annexure-I):

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG)
  • Signature with black ink on white paper (140×60 pixels; 10 KB–20 KB; JPEG)
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with black or blue ink (240×240 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG/JPG)
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with black ink (800×400 pixels; 50 KB–100 KB; JPEG/JPG) — in candidate's own handwriting
  • Live photograph captured via webcam or mobile phone (to be uploaded at the time of online application)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Examination / Document Verification / Engagement:

  • Call Letter (downloaded from Bank's website) along with photo identity proof (Passport / Aadhaar / PAN Card / Driving License / Voter's Card / Bank Passbook with photograph / Identity Card issued by School or College / Gazetted Officer in official letterhead) in original and self-attested photocopy
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST candidates) — to be submitted at the time of engagement if called for
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate in prescribed Government of India format, issued during the period 01-04-2026 to the date of joining (for OBC candidates)
  • Income and Asset Certificate issued by Competent Authority in the format prescribed by Government of India, valid for the year 2026-27 (for EWS candidates)
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by a Competent Authority as per Government of India guidelines (for PwBD candidates)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marksheets (Graduation onwards)

Printout of Application Form is NOT to be sent to SBI. No Hard Copy of the Call Letter for Online Written Examination will be sent by post. Candidates must appear for the test at their own cost; SBI will not pay or reimburse any such expenses.

Apply Online at: https://www.sbi.co.in/careers or https://sbi.bank.in/careers

For queries: Call 022-22820427 (11:00 AM to 06:00 PM on working days) or visit https://cgrs.ibps.in — mention "Engagement of Apprentice in SBI" in the subject.

Important Instructions for SBI Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • THIS IS NOT AN EMPLOYMENT IN THE BANK. NOR IS THE SAME CONTRACT EMPLOYMENT. Candidates engaged as Apprentice will NOT be treated as "employees" of SBI and will not be entitled to receive any benefits available for employees of the Bank.
  • SBI shall have no obligation to offer regular employment to Apprentices during and/or after the completion of the Apprenticeship period. After completion, candidates will be relieved from the respective work area.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement in one State only and can appear for the examination only once under this engagement project.
  • Mandatory registration on NAPS portal (https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before applying online on SBI website is required.
  • No Ex-Servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an Apprentice and there is no reservation for Ex-Servicemen in the Apprentice Engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the "Creamy Layer" are not entitled to reservation/relaxation; they should apply as General or General (LD/VI/HI/d&e) as applicable.
  • Change of category will not be permitted after registration of online application.
  • Candidates with CIBIL default record (unpaid loans/credit card dues) are not eligible for engagement until dues are regularized/repaid.
  • All candidates (except Northeastern State candidates) must produce Aadhaar Number before engagement/contract signing.
  • Not more than one application should be submitted; in case of multiple applications, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • PwBD candidates with disability conditions falling under "likely to improve" (temporary disability) category are not eligible for reservation.
  • PwBD candidates eligible for scribe facility: candidates with 40% or more disability having physical limitation to write are eligible. Scribe must be arranged by the candidate at his/her own cost. Compensatory time of 20 minutes per hour of examination is allowed (whether availing scribe or not) for eligible PwBD candidates.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their registered email ID/mobile active for receiving call letters/advices etc.
  • Mobile phones, smartwatches, pagers, earphones or any other electronic/communication devices are not allowed inside the examination premises.
  • All revisions/corrigendum (if any) will be hosted on the Bank's website only — candidates should visit regularly.
  • No request for change of examination centre shall be entertained. No Travel Expenses will be reimbursed/paid by SBI.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.
  • Bank reserves right to cancel the selection process partly/entirely at any stage.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Get the latest BOB recruitment notification 2026 for 5000 Apprentice vacancies under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The online application process starts on 19th May 2026 and the last date to apply online is 8th June 2026. Find Bank of Baroda Apprentice vacancies across all States/UTs on the official website http://bankofbaroda.bank.in. You can also get OnlyJobs of "Upcoming Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026" notifications for freshers and experienced candidates. Also know the direct official link for BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 apply online from the Bank of Baroda career page.

FOLLOW US

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Bank of Baroda has officially released Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026 for engagement of 5000 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961 (as amended from time to time). The notification includes complete details on training seats, eligibility, selection process, State/UT-wise vacancy breakup, PwBD reservation, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying. The notification PDF is available on the official Bank of Baroda website and BFSI SSC's website.

  • Download Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name Bank of Baroda (BOB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. BOB/HRM/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2026/01 dated 19/05/2026
Post Name Apprentice (under Apprentices Act, 1961)

Stipend Rs. 15,000/- per month (no other allowances/benefits)

Online Examination Date To be notified on Bank's website and https://bfsissc.com
Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test Date To be notified after Online Examination result

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 5000 training seats have been announced for engagement as Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. These are distributed State/UT-wise across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories with horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of training seats is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.

Multiple Disabilities Disability amongst clauses above including deaf-blindness in the posts identified for each disability

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Candidates must be registered on Government Apprenticeship Portals (NATS and/or NAPS) before applying. Only Indian citizens are eligible to apply.

Criteria Details
Registration Requirement Candidates must be registered on NAPS portal (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and/or NATS portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) before applying.
Educational Qualification Graduation Degree in any Discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualifications recognized by the Central Government. For NATS candidates: date of passing Graduation should not be more than 4 years preceding 01-05-2026.
Prior Apprenticeship Candidate should NOT have undergone Apprenticeship with Bank of Baroda or any other Establishment in the past, and should not currently be pursuing Apprenticeship Training with any Establishment.
Training / Job Experience Candidates who had training or job experience for a period of 1 year or more after completing educational qualification shall NOT be eligible.
State/UT Application Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only and must select up to 3 preferred training locations/centres from those notified within that State/UT.
Nationality Indian Citizens only.

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be a minimum of 20 years and maximum of 28 years of age as on the cut-off date (01-05-2026). Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for reserved categories, subject to the condition that NAPS-registered candidates should be below 34 years as on 01-05-2026.

Category Age Relaxation (in years)
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe 5
Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer) 3
PwBD – General / EWS 10
PwBD – OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 13
PwBD – SC / ST 15

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay non-refundable Application/Intimation/Examination Charges/Fees to BFSI SSC through the secure payment link provided in the application form. The fee must be paid through ONLINE mode only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account.

Category Total Fee / Charges
Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
Person with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) Candidates Rs. 200/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Male Candidates Rs. 800/- plus GST
General / EWS / OBC – Female / Transgender Candidates Rs. 600/- plus GST

BOB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection for engagement of apprentices will be based on three stages - Online Examination, Document Verification, and Test of local language of the State. Candidates must qualify all three stages. Final selection is subject to medical fitness as per the Bank's requirements. A State-wise and Category-wise Wait List will be published for vacancies arising from non-acceptance or non-reporting by selected candidates; this wait list remains valid for 1 year from result declaration or until the next engagement exercise, whichever is earlier.

Stage 1 - Online Examination

S No Name of Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Medium of Test
1 General / Financial Awareness 25 25 60 Minutes Hindi / English
2 Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude 25 25 Hindi / English
3 Computer Knowledge 25 25 Hindi / English
4 General English 25 25 English
Total 100 100  

There will be NO negative marks for wrong answers. The online examination will be remotely proctored - candidates can appear from any location of their choice using a smart mobile phone / laptop / desktop / tablet with an operational front camera, microphone, and stable Wi-Fi internet connection. SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates get 5% relaxation in minimum qualifying marks on aggregate. Merit list will be drawn State-wise and Category-wise in descending order of aggregate marks. Tie-break: higher age is preferred.

Stage 2 - Document Verification

Candidates qualifying the online examination will be called for Document Verification at a Bank-designated centre of the State/UT applied for, at their own expense. Bank of Baroda will not reimburse travelling expenses for Document Verification.

Stage 3 - Test of Local Language (State-wise)

How to Apply for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only. No other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must first mandatorily register themselves on the Government Apprenticeship portals - NATS (https://nats.education.gov.in) and/or NAPS (https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in).

After completing registration on NAPS/NATS, eligible candidates will receive an email from [email protected] inviting them to fill the final "Application cum Examination Form" and make payment of the applicable fee/charges.

The application registration is considered complete only when fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC. Candidates must note down their Enrolment Id (NATS portal) and Apprentice Registration Code (NAPS portal) for all future correspondence.

Documents Required for Registration on NATS/NAPS Portals:

  • Colour scan copy of Aadhaar Card – Front & Back Page (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of PAN Card (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Valid Personal Email ID and Mobile Number
  • Colour scan copy of Passport-size Photograph (JPEG, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Xth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of XIIth Marksheet (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Degree Marksheet / Provisional Certificate (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Saving Bank Passbook / Cheque Leaf (PDF, <1 MB)
  • Colour scan copy of Signature (JPEG, <1 MB)

Documents to be Produced at the time of Document Verification / Language Proficiency Test:

  • Printout of valid Language Proficiency Test Intimation Letter/mail from Bank
  • Valid system generated printout of the online application / Registration / Enrollment form
  • Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate from Competent Municipal Authority or SSLC / Std. X Certificate with DOB)
  • Photo Identity Proof (Government-issued ID)
  • Individual Semester/Year-wise Marksheets and Certificates for educational qualifications including final degree/diploma certificate
  • CGPA to Percentage conversion certificate (if grades awarded), issued by the appropriate authority
  • Caste Certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for SC/ST/OBC/EWS candidates) - OBC-NCL certificate must be issued within one year as on date of advertisement
  • Disability Certificate in prescribed format issued by District Medical Board (for PwBD candidates)
  • Any other relevant documents in support of eligibility

Documents to be produced at the venue of Document Verification / Language Test. Non-submission of requisite documents will debar candidature from further participation in the selection process. Candidates must appear at their own expense.

Apply Online at: https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/ and https://bfsissc.com

Important Instructions for Bank of Baroda Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • This is NOT regular employment or contract employment in the Bank. Apprentices will be TRAINEES only and not employees of Bank of Baroda.
  • The apprentice shall have no right to claim employment in the Bank after completion of training. Bank of Baroda has no obligation to offer regular employment to apprentices during or after the apprenticeship period.
  • Candidates can apply for engagement as apprentice in one State/UT only. Allotment to a different State/UT will not be considered under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must first register on NATS and/or NAPS portals before applying; application without portal registration is not valid.
  • The process of registration is complete only when applicable fee/charges are deposited with BFSI SSC.
  • No Ex-servicemen / Disabled Ex-Servicemen shall be engaged as an apprentice; there is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in this engagement.
  • OBC candidates belonging to the 'Creamy Layer' are not entitled to OBC reservation and age relaxation; they should apply as General / General (OC/HI/VI/ID) as applicable.
  • Change of category once submitted in the online application will not be permitted at any stage.
  • Candidates should submit only one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be considered and fee paid for earlier registrations will be forfeited.
  • The Apprentices are prohibited from joining / forming any Union Body.
  • All correspondence will be made only on the registered email ID; candidates should keep it active throughout the recruitment process. No hard copy intimation will be sent by post for Online Examination.
  • Candidates must appear for the selection process (Document Verification, Language Test) at their own cost; Bank will not reimburse travelling expenses.
  • Apprentices will not be provided with Transport / Hostel facility or any other residential accommodation during training.
  • Stipend of Rs. 15,000/- per month will be paid on completion of each month after adjusting Loss of Pay, if any. No other allowances/benefits are payable.
  • All amendments/corrigendum (if any) will be published only on the Bank's website (https://bankofbaroda.bank.in/) and https://bfsissc.com - candidates should visit regularly.
  • Any canvassing directly or indirectly shall disqualify the candidature. Use of unfair means, impersonation, or misbehavior will lead to disqualification and/or debarment.
  • Legal jurisdiction for any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be Mumbai only.

The State Bank of India (SBI) has officially announced the Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) - Specialist Cadre Officer Recruitment 2026 with 100 openings. The detailed notification was published on 13 May 2026 at sbi.co.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 23 to 32 years (relaxations applicable as per category) and hold a Graduate degree in any discipline from a recognized University along with Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF can now submit their online applications from 13 May 2026 to 02 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below.

FOLLOW US

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

State Bank of India, Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has officially released Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05 dated 13.05.2026 for recruitment to 100 posts of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) on Regular Basis under Specialist Cadre Officers. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, age limit, pay scale, job profile, selection process, application fee, and instructions to apply. The position shall remain in Specialist cadre and will have no provision for conversion into General cadre. Candidates are strongly advised to carefully read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the Bank’s official career website.

SBI Trade Finance Officer SCO Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai
Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05 dated 13.05.2026
Post Name Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) – Specialist Cadre Officer, Regular Basis

Total Vacancies 100 (Regular: 88 + Backlog: 12)

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 100 vacancies have been announced for the post of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) on regular basis, comprising 88 regular vacancies and 12 backlog vacancies. The vacancies include horizontal reservations for PwBD candidates. EWS vacancies are tentative and subject to further directives of Government of India and outcome of any litigation. The position shall remain in Specialist cadre with no provision for conversion into General cadre.

Post Type Total
Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) Regular 88
Backlog 12
Total 100

SC: Scheduled Caste | ST: Scheduled Tribe | OBC: Other Backward Class | EWS: Economically Weaker Section | UR: Unreserved | VI: Visually Impaired | HI: Hearing Impaired | LD: Locomotive Disability | d&e: Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness, Autism spectrum disorder, Multiple disabilities. PwBD reservation is horizontal and included in overall vacancy of respective parent category.

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 30.04.2026. Teaching and training experience will not be counted for eligibility. The relevant experience certificate from the employer must specifically confirm that the candidate had experience in the related field as required. In cases where the degree/diploma certificate does not specify the field of specialization, a certificate from the concerned university/college specifically mentioning the specialization must be produced.

Educational Qualification and Experience

Qualification / Experience Details
Mandatory Educational Qualification Graduate (any discipline) from Government recognized University or Institution AND Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF (Certificate should be dated on or before 30.04.2026)
Preferred Qualification Certificate for Documentary Credit Specialists (CDCS) / Certificate in Trade Finance / Certificate in International Banking (Certificate should be dated on or before 30.04.2026)
Mandatory Experience Minimum 2 years’ post-essential academic qualification experience in Trade Finance processing in Supervisory/Official role in any Scheduled Commercial Bank
Specific Skills Required Outstanding communication, presentation and processing skills

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Age is calculated as on 30.04.2026. Cumulative age relaxation will not be available. Candidates seeking age relaxation are required to submit copies of necessary certificate(s) at the time of interview, if shortlisted. No change in category of any candidate is permitted after online registration; no correspondence/email/phone will be entertained in this regard.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
Unreserved (UR) 23 to 32 years (as on 30.04.2026)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation
SC / ST 5 years relaxation
PwBD (UR / EWS) 10 years relaxation
PwBD (OBC) 13 years relaxation
PwBD (SC / ST) 15 years relaxation

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fee and Intimation Charges are non-refundable and must be paid online through the payment gateway integrated with the application (Debit Card/Credit Card/Internet Banking). Transaction charges for online payment, if any, will be borne by the candidate. Application fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be adjusted for any other examination or selection in future. In case of multiple applications, fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / OBC Rs. 750/- (Rupees Seven Hundred Fifty only)
SC / ST / PwBD Nil (No fee / intimation charges)

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be on the basis of Shortlisting and Interview. Merely fulfilling the minimum qualification and experience will not vest any right to candidate for being called for interview. The shortlisting committee constituted by the Bank will decide the shortlisting parameters. The decision of the Bank to call the candidates for interview shall be final. Interview will carry 100 marks; qualifying marks will be decided by the Bank. Merit list for selection will be prepared in descending order on the basis of scores obtained in interview only. In case of tie, candidates will be ranked according to their age in descending order. Intimation/Call Letter for Interview will be sent by email or uploaded on Bank’s website only - no hard copy will be sent.

Stage Details
Stage 1 Shortlisting (based on qualification, suitability, and experience as decided by the Bank’s shortlisting committee)
Stage 2 Interview (100 marks; qualifying marks decided by the Bank)
Merit List Prepared in descending order of interview scores; tie-breaking by age in descending order
Probation Period 6 months (as per Bank’s extant recruitment policy)

Grade, Pay Scale & Perquisites

Grade Probation Period Pay Scale Perquisites
Middle Management Grade Scale – II (MMGS-II) 6 Months Rs. 64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960 DA, HRA, CCA, Provident Fund, NPS (Contributory Pension Fund), Leave Fare Concession (LFC), Medical Facility, and other perquisites as per rules in force from time to time

SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 - Job Profile & Key Responsibility Areas

The Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) position is in the Specialist cadre with no provision for conversion into General cadre. Suggested place of posting is Hyderabad and Kolkata; however, selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India at the sole discretion of the Bank. Requests for transfer/allotment to any centre other than Kolkata/Hyderabad shall not be entertained.

Area Details
Job Profile Trade Finance Document Scrutiny (LC/Collections/Bank Guarantees) for compliance with Bank/RBI/ICC/FEDAI/regulatory guidelines; Processing of Trade Finance and Forex transactions at a centralized location; Reconciliation of Trade Finance and Forex related entries in Bank’s Trade Finance, Forex and CBS systems and IDPMS/EDPMS; Any other tasks related to Bank’s Trade Finance and International Business back-office operations.
Key Responsibility Areas Accurate and efficient processing of all allotted Trade Finance and/or Forex transactions within benchmarked TAT; Tracking and ensuring periodic activities including maturities, payments, prompt follow-up, customer reconciliation; Timely reconciliation of related accounts/systems; Work closely with all stakeholders to maintain agreed customer TAT; Ensure adherence to all regulatory/bank/ICC/FEDAI guidelines; Participation in RCSA, BCOR, process review and training activities; Timely submission/compliance of all Audit Reports; Any other activity as directed by Team Leaders and Department/Branch Heads.

How to Apply for SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the link available on SBI’s official website https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings. No other mode of application will be entertained. Hard copy of application and other documents need not be sent to this office. The process of registration is completed only when fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date. Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date and not to wait till the last date to avoid any technical issues.

Candidates must upload all required documents at the time of online application; failure to upload documents will result in candidature not being considered for shortlisting/interview. After expiry of the window for online application, no change/correction/modification will be allowed under any circumstances.

Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Recent Photograph & Signature (JPEG)
  • Detailed Resume (PDF)
  • ID Proof (PDF)
  • Proof of Date of Birth (PDF)
  • Caste Certificate & PwBD Certificate (if applicable) (PDF)
  • Mandatory Educational Certificates: Degree Certificate (PDF)
  • Mandatory Certification in Forex Operations from IIBF (PDF)
  • Experience Certificates (mandatory) (PDF)
  • Preferred Certificate, if any (PDF)
  • Biodata Form (duly filled, signed & scanned - available on Bank’s career website under the advertisement) (PDF)
  • Form-16 / Offer Letter / Latest Salary Slip from current employer (PDF)
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC) from present employer (mandatory if working in Government Institute/Public Sector Unit/Public Sector Bank) (PDF)
  • Mandatory Undertaking (format available on Bank’s career page against Adv. No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/05) (PDF)

Apply Online at: https://sbi.co.in/web/careers/current-openings

Important Instructions for SBI Trade Finance Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Candidates must apply online only; hard copy of application and other documents need not be sent to SBI’s office.
  • The position of Trade Finance Officer (MMGS-II) shall remain in Specialist cadre and will have no provision for conversion into General cadre.
  • Teaching and training experience will not be counted for eligibility.
  • Candidates with any disciplinary proceedings pending or contemplated against them as on date will not be eligible to apply; an undertaking must be uploaded at the time of online application.
  • CIBIL: Candidates who have defaulted in repayment under any lending arrangement with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions including credit card dues and have not regularized/repaid their outstanding till the date of issuance of offer of recruitment by the Bank shall not be eligible for appointment.
  • Candidates serving in Govt./Quasi Govt. offices/Public Sector undertakings/Nationalized Banks must submit NOC from their employer at the time of interview, failing which their candidature will not be considered.
  • In case of multiple applications for the same post, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained; fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited.
  • OBC certificate containing the ‘non-creamy layer’ clause, issued during the period 01.04.2026 to the date of interview, must be submitted by OBC candidates if called for interview.
  • EWS candidates not in possession of “Income & Asset Certificate” for FY2025-26 on or before closure of online application date should apply under “UR Category” only.
  • Outstation candidates called for interview will be reimbursed airfare (economy class, shortest route, to-and-fro) up to a maximum of Rs. 10,000/- on submission of original flight tickets/invoice and boarding pass.
  • All changes/updates/revisions/corrigendum/results will be hosted on the Bank’s website only; no separate intimation will be issued.
  • Any legal proceedings arising out of this advertisement can be instituted only in Mumbai; Courts/Tribunals/Forums at Mumbai only shall have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.
  • Candidates are advised to keep their email ID/mobile number active till the declaration of result and issuance of call letters on final selection.
  • SBI does not endorse, authorize or associate with any external coaching platform, consultancy, individual or digital channel claiming to provide guaranteed selection or insider guidance. Candidates must rely solely on information available on SBI’s official career portal.

  1. Solution 1: Clear browser cache and cookies, then try again with a fresh session
  2. Solution 2: Try different web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Microsoft Edge, Safari)
  3. Solution 3: Check your application status on the official website
  4. Solution 4: Contact RBI helpdesk immediately

Important: Act immediately if facing issues downloading admit card. Don't wait until exam day. Contact RBI helpdesk at least 2 days before the exam.

Stay Updated: Bookmark reliable sources for latest updates on RBI Office Attendant Result 2026, answer key, and all recruitment notifications.

The latest Export-Import Bank of India (Exim Bank) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Deputy Manager (Information Security) posts! Published vide Advertisement No. HRM/DM/2026-27/03, this new recruitment drive offers 4 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants with a minimum 60% in full-time B.E./B.Tech./M.Tech./M.E./M.C.A. in Computer Science/IT/Electronics & Communication from a recognized University/Institute, with minimum 1 year of relevant experience, and aged between 21 to 28 years (as on 01-05-2026) can apply online from 26-05-2026 to 14-06-2026. Find all details about the Exim Bank Deputy Manager new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

Export-Import Bank of India (Exim Bank), an all-India premier financial institution engaged in financing, facilitating and promoting India's international trade, has officially released Advertisement No. HRM/DM/2026-27/03 for Direct Recruitment of Deputy Managers (Information Security). The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility criteria, age limits, salary, selection process, examination pattern, application fee, and application instructions. Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 4 Deputy Manager (Information Security) vacancies have been announced under Advertisement No. HRM/DM/2026-27/03. Reservations are applicable as per prevailing Government of India guidelines. The Bank reserves the right to cancel/restrict/curtail/enlarge the recruitment process or revise the number of vacancies without notice.

Position UR OBC (NCL) Total
Deputy Manager (Information Security) 03 01 04

Abbreviations: UR – Unreserved, OBC (NCL) – Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer). Note: Reservations for SC/ST/EWS/PwBD as applicable per prevailing GoI guidelines. Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in India as per Bank's requirement and may be transferred to offices outside India with consent.

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions (qualification, experience, and age) as on the prescribed dates. Only Indian Citizens are eligible to apply. Candidates having completed their graduation/post-graduation through correspondence/part-time/open university/open learning/external/distance learning are NOT eligible. For CGPA-based grading, candidates must provide a conversion letter from the Head of Institute/Controller of Examination if the equivalent percentage is not mentioned on the marksheet/certificate.

Criteria Details
Nationality Indian Citizens only.
Essential Academic Qualification Minimum 60% aggregate marks / equivalent CGPA in Full-time B.E./B.Tech./M.Tech./M.E./M.C.A. in Computer Science / Information Technology / Electronics & Communication from a University/Institute recognized/approved by Government/AICTE/UGC.
Essential Experience Minimum 1 year of experience in a Scheduled Commercial Bank or All India Financial Institution or any other organization of repute, in Information Security functions.
Preferred Certifications Preference given to candidates possessing any one of: CISA / CISM / CISSP / OSCP / CEH / CISP / CRTP / CCNA / CCNP / ISO 27001:2022 LA/LI / CompTIA Security+.
Additional Skills Excellent communication skills and proficiency in computers and information technology.
Not Eligible Candidates who completed graduation/post-graduation through correspondence/part-time/open university/open learning/external/distance learning are not eligible.
OBC Non-Creamy Layer OBC certificate must specifically contain a clause of non-creamy layer exclusion from GoI reservation benefits. Certificate must be valid as on last date of applying (issued within one year prior to last date). Caste name must tally letter by letter with Central Government list. OBC Creamy Layer candidates and those whose caste is not in Central List must apply as General.
Government Employees Candidates already in service must produce a proper relieving letter/discharge certificate in original from present employer at the time of joining, failing which they will not be allowed to join.
Service Bond Selected candidates must execute a personal bond of Rs. 3 lakh to serve the Bank for a minimum period of 5 years from date of joining.

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be not below 21 years and not above 28 years as on 01-05-2026. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per Government of India guidelines. OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates must apply under General category and are not entitled to age relaxation.

Sr. No. Category Maximum Age (as on 01-05-2026)
1 General (UR) / EWS 28 years
2 OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 31 years (3 years relaxation)
3 Persons with Disabilities (PwBDs) 10 years relaxation over General limit
4 Ex-Servicemen 5 years relaxation (as per DOPT circular No. 36034/2/2013 dated 08-04-2013, for Group A direct recruitment through All India Competitive Examination)

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Application fees and intimation charges are non-refundable. The process of registration is complete only when fee is deposited through online mode on or before the last date i.e. 14-06-2026. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate. Fee once paid will not be refunded even if the applicant is not found eligible.

Category Application Fee / Intimation Charges
General / OBC (NCL) Rs. 600/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD / EWS / Female candidates (all communities) Rs. 100/- (Intimation Charges only - Non-Refundable)

Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

Selection will be based on Written Test followed by Personal Interview. Candidates shortlisted based on written test performance will be called for Personal Interview. Final selection will be based on overall performance in both stages. Written Examination carries 70% weightage and Personal Interview carries 30% weightage in the final merit list. There will be no sectional cut-off in the written test. Exim Bank will analyse responses to detect patterns of similarity - if copying is inferred, candidature will be cancelled.

Stage 1 - Written Test (Professional Knowledge - Objective Type)

Name of the Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
Professional Knowledge - Objective (Part I: Information Technology and Cyber Security) 100 100 2 Hours 30 Minutes

Candidates may be required to be at the venue for about 4 hours (including time for document verification, instructions, etc.). No request for change of centre/venue/date/session will be entertained. Examination centres: Kolkata, Mumbai, New Delhi, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad (may change if sufficient candidates not available). Personal Interviews will be conducted only in Mumbai and New Delhi. Written test carries 70% weightage; Interview carries 30% weightage in final merit.

Stage 2 - Personal Interview

Candidates shortlisted on the basis of written test performance will be called for Personal Interview at Mumbai or New Delhi. Candidates must produce original degree certificates, caste, experience and other relevant certificates at the time of interview. Candidates already in service must carry a proper relieving letter/discharge certificate from their present employer at the time of joining.

Exim Bank Deputy Manager (Information Security) Recruitment 2026 - Pre-Recruitment Training

A pre-recruitment training will be organized for candidates from reserved categories. Details of the training and its mode of delivery (online/offline) will be intimated to candidates who opt for it through the online application form. Details will be shared after the last date of registration.

Who should NOT apply for Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026?

  • Candidates who do not hold a full-time B.E./B.Tech./M.Tech./M.E./M.C.A. in Computer Science/IT/Electronics & Communication - degrees from other disciplines or part-time/distance/correspondence courses are strictly ineligible.
  • Candidates with aggregate marks below 60% in their qualifying degree - the 60% minimum is a hard eligibility condition.
  • Candidates who do not have at least 1 year of post-qualification experience in Information Security functions in a Scheduled Commercial Bank, All India Financial Institution, or other organization of repute - experience in unrelated IT roles does not qualify.
  • Candidates outside the age band of 21 to 28 years as on 01-05-2026 (except those entitled to OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen relaxation).
  • OBC Creamy Layer candidates expecting OBC reservation or age relaxation - they must apply as General.
  • Candidates not willing to serve anywhere in India - posting is at the Bank's requirement at any location in India including transfers to overseas offices with consent.
  • Candidates who cannot commit to the 5-year service bond of Rs. 3 lakh - this bond is mandatory and must be executed at the time of joining.
  • Candidates currently in government service who cannot produce a proper relieving letter/discharge certificate from their employer at the time of joining - they will not be allowed to join without it.

Common mistakes / What to check before applying Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026?

  • Uploading a signature in CAPITAL LETTERS - capital letter signatures are not accepted and will lead to rejection.
  • Not writing the handwritten declaration personally in English - if written by anyone else or in any other language, the application will be declared invalid.
  • Not uploading a live photograph via webcam/mobile at the time of filling the application - this is mandatory in addition to the static scanned photograph.
  • Uploading blurry, smudged, or unclear photograph, signature, left thumb impression, or handwritten declaration - such applications may be rejected; candidates should preview all uploads before final submission.
  • Not cross-checking that the name in the application exactly matches the name on the photo identity proof - any mismatch leads to denial of entry at the examination/interview; female candidates who have changed name post-marriage must be especially careful.
  • Clicking Back or Refresh during online fee payment - this may cause double charges; wait for server intimation after payment submission.
  • Not taking a printout of the e-Receipt and online application form after fee payment - this is required for future reference.
  • Not keeping the email ID and mobile number active till the end of the recruitment process - all communications including admit card, call letters, and results are sent only via email/SMS.
  • Not furnishing the CGPA conversion formula/letter from University/Institute if degree is CGPA-based - without this, the equivalent percentage cannot be verified and candidature may be rejected.
  • Waiting till the last date to apply - the Bank is not responsible for website outages or connectivity issues; candidates must apply well before 14-06-2026.

How to Apply for Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must apply online only through the link available on the Bank's Careers page at https://www.eximbankindia.in/careers. The application link will be live from 26-05-2026. No other means or mode of application will be accepted. Before applying, candidates must scan and keep ready all required documents. The application registration is complete only when fee is successfully paid online on or before 14-06-2026.

Documents to be Scanned and Uploaded before applying:

  • Recent passport-size colour photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm; 200×230 pixels preferred; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG) - light/white background, no caps, dark glasses or religious headwear covering face
  • Live photograph - to be captured via webcam or mobile phone QR scan at the time of filling the application form (mandatory in addition to static photo)
  • Signature with Black Ink on white paper (140×60 pixels preferred; 10 KB–20 KB; JPEG/JPG) - NOT in CAPITAL LETTERS
  • Left Thumb Impression on white paper with Black or Blue ink (240×240 pixels in 200 DPI preferred; 20 KB–50 KB; JPEG/JPG)
  • Handwritten Declaration in English on white paper with Black ink (800×400 pixels in 200 DPI preferred; 50 KB–100 KB; JPEG/JPG) - in candidate's own handwriting only
  • Consolidated Mark Sheets of Graduation & Post-Graduation (PDF; 20 KB–500 KB)
  • Graduation & Post-Graduation Degree Certificate (PDF; 20 KB–500 KB)
  • Caste Certificate - if applicable (PDF; 20 KB–500 KB)

Documents to be Produced in Original at the time of Written Examination / Interview:

  • Call Letter (downloaded from Bank's website) with original and photocopy of currently valid photo identity proof bearing exactly the same name as on the call letter - valid IDs: PAN Card, Passport, Permanent Driving License, Voter's Card, Bank Passbook with photograph, Photo identity proof issued by Gazetted Officer on official letterhead, Photo identity proof issued by People's Representative on official letterhead, valid College/University Identity Card, Aadhaar Card/E-Aadhaar with photograph, Employee ID, or Bar Council Identity Card. (Note: Ration Card and Learner's Driving License are NOT valid.)
  • Original degree certificates, caste certificate, experience certificates, and any other relevant certificates (at the time of interview)
  • In case of name change - original Gazette notification, original marriage certificate, or affidavit in original
  • CGPA conversion letter from Head of Institute/Controller of Examination (if CGPA-based grading)
  • Scribe undertaking and disability/medical certificate (for PwBD candidates requesting scribe facility - must be registered for scribe and compensatory time in online application form)

The handwritten declaration text is: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."

Candidates are advised to take a printout of the system-generated online application form after registration. No change/alteration is possible after clicking COMPLETE REGISTRATION. Any legal proceedings can be instituted only in Mumbai - Mumbai courts/tribunals/forums shall have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.

Apply Online at: https://www.eximbankindia.in/careers

Important Instructions for Exim Bank Deputy Manager Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Application link will be live from 26-05-2026 and closes on 14-06-2026 - apply well in advance without waiting for the last date.
  • Registration is complete only when fee is paid online on or before 14-06-2026. Fee once paid is non-refundable under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must carry the call letter along with original and a photocopy of currently valid photo identity proof to the examination and interview - without these, entry will not be permitted.
  • The name on the call letter must exactly match the name on the photo identity proof - female candidates who have changed name post-marriage must note this carefully.
  • Candidates reporting late after the reporting time on the call letter will NOT be permitted to take the examination.
  • No request for change of examination centre/venue/date/session will be entertained.
  • Mobile phones (even in switched-off mode), pagers, or any electronic communication devices are strictly prohibited in the examination/interview hall - violation leads to disqualification and possible debarment from future Exim Bank recruitments.
  • Exim Bank will analyse response patterns - if response sharing is detected, candidature will be cancelled and results withheld.
  • Pre-recruitment training will be organized for reserved category candidates who opt for it in the online application form.
  • PwBD candidates requiring a scribe must arrange their own scribe at their own cost and must indicate the requirement in the online application form - subsequent requests may not be entertained. Eligible PwBD/locomotor disability/visually impaired candidates get 20 minutes of compensatory time per hour.
  • Selected candidates must execute a personal bond of Rs. 3 lakh to serve the Bank for a minimum of 5 years from date of joining.
  • Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in India as per Bank's requirement and may be transferred to overseas offices with consent.
  • Candidates already in service must produce a relieving letter/discharge certificate from present employer at the time of joining - without it, joining will not be permitted.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification.
  • All matters regarding eligibility, selection process, results, etc. - the Bank's decision is final and binding. No correspondence will be entertained from ineligible or non-selected candidates.
  • Legal proceedings can only be instituted in Mumbai - Mumbai courts/tribunals/forums have sole and exclusive jurisdiction.

How to Check RRB Paramedical Cut Off Marks 2026 Online?

Follow these simple steps to download the official cut off notification:

Last Date for Online Application Submission 14-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Last Date for Application Fee Payment 16-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Modification Window for Application Corrections 17-06-2026 to 26-06-2026 (upto 23:59 Hrs.)
Age Calculated As On 01-07-2026
CBT-1 / CBT-2 / CBAT Date To be notified on official RRB websites

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 11127 vacancies have been announced for the post of Assistant Loco Pilot across all Railway Recruitment Boards and Zonal Railways. These are distributed across UR, SC, ST, OBC, and EWS categories. Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) vacancies are included within the total vacancy count and are not separately additional. The post is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD).

Category No. of Posts
UR (Unreserved) 4860
SC (Scheduled Caste) 1746
ST (Scheduled Tribe) 919
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 2598
EWS (Economically Weaker Section) 1004
Total 11127
Ex-Servicemen (ExSM) - included in above total 1115

RRB & Zone-wise Vacancy Details (Selected RRBs)

RRB Zone UR SC ST OBC EWS Total ExSM
Ahmedabad WR 62 22 11 38 14 147 15
Ajmer NWR + WCR 318 115 59 174 52 718 72
Mumbai CR + SCR + WR 336 219 106 380 152 1193 120
Bhubaneswar ECoR 548 70 36 48 99 801 80
Guwahati NFR 272 109 63 164 68 676 68
Secunderabad ECoR + SCR 625 209 102 390 94 1420 143
Ranchi ECR + SER 472 233 105 363 127 1300 130
All RRBs Total 4860 1746 919 2598 1004 11127 1115

Note: Complete RRB & Zone-wise vacancy details are available in Annexure B of the official notification.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of online application (14-06-2026). Educational/Technical qualification certificates must be from a recognized Board/University/Institute. Candidates awaiting results of their final examination for the prescribed qualification are NOT eligible to apply. The post of ALP is not suitable for Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD). Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for this post.

Educational Qualification

Option Details
Option A - Matriculation + ITI Matriculation / SSLC plus ITI from recognised institutions of NCVT/SCVT in the trades of Fitter, Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV), Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Wireman, Tractor Mechanic, Armature & Coil Winder, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration & Air-Conditioning Mechanic. OR Matriculation / SSLC plus Course Completed Act Apprenticeship in the above trades.
Option B - Matriculation + Diploma/Degree in Engineering Matriculation / SSLC plus 3-year Diploma in Mechanical / Electrical / Electronics / Automobile Engineering (or combination of these streams) from a recognised institution in lieu of ITI. Note: Degree in the above Engineering disciplines will also be acceptable in lieu of Diploma.

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be between 18 and 30 years of age as on 01-07-2026. Age relaxation is available for various categories as detailed below. If a candidate is eligible for relaxation on more than one ground, only the maximum relaxation will be granted (not cumulative). SC, ST, and OBC (NCL) candidates applying against UR vacancies will not get any age relaxation. There is no fixed limit on the number of attempts - candidates can appear as long as they are within the prescribed upper age limit.

Category Age Limit / Relaxation
General (UR) & EWS 18 to 30 years (as on 01-07-2026)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) 3 years relaxation (max 33 years)
SC & ST 5 years relaxation (max 35 years)
Ex-Servicemen (UR & EWS) 3 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (OBC-NCL) 6 years after deduction of length of service from age
Ex-Servicemen (SC & ST) 8 years after deduction of length of service from age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - UR & EWS Maximum 40 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - OBC (NCL) Maximum 43 years of age
Serving Railway Staff (Group C/D) - SC & ST Maximum 45 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (UR & EWS) Maximum 35 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (OBC-NCL) Maximum 38 years of age
Widowed / Divorced / Judicially Separated Women (SC & ST) Maximum 40 years of age

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the non-refundable recruitment fee online. Fee can be paid only through online mode - internet banking, debit/credit cards, or UPI. Applications received without the prescribed fee will be summarily rejected. Candidates who appear in CBT-1 will receive a refund of their examination fee (after deducting applicable bank charges) credited to their Aadhaar-seeded bank account. Candidates whose applications are rejected, or who do not appear in CBT-1, will not receive any refund.

Category Examination Fee Refund on Appearing in CBT-1
All Candidates (except categories below) Rs. 500/- (Rupees Five Hundred only) Rs. 400/- refunded (deducting bank charges)
SC / ST / Ex-Servicemen / Female / Transgender / Minorities / Economically Backward Class (EBC) Rs. 250/- (Rupees Two Hundred and Fifty only) Rs. 250/- fully refunded (deducting bank charges)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 consists of five successive stages - First Stage CBT (CBT-1), Second Stage CBT (CBT-2), Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT), Document Verification (DV), and Medical Examination (ME). CBT-1 is a screening exam only and its marks are not counted in the final panel. The final merit list is prepared on the basis of combined marks from Part-A of CBT-2 (50% weightage) and CBAT scores (50% weightage). Negative marking of 1/3rd mark per wrong answer applies in both CBT-1 and CBT-2. There is no negative marking in CBAT.

Stage 1 - First Stage CBT (CBT-1) - Screening Only

Details Particulars
Duration 60 Minutes
Total Questions / Marks 75 Questions / 75 Marks (1 mark per question)
Type Objective MCQ - Computer Based Test
Negative Marking 1/3rd mark deducted per wrong answer
Minimum Qualifying Marks UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Shortlisting for CBT-2 15 times the vacancies notified per RRB (based on CBT-1 merit, RRB-wise)
Syllabus Mathematics, Mental Ability (Reasoning), General Science (10th standard level)

Stage 2 - Second Stage CBT (CBT-2)

Part Duration Questions Nature Minimum Qualifying Marks
Part-A 90 Minutes 100 Questions / 100 Marks Competitive - marks counted for merit UR & EWS: 40% | OBC (NCL): 30% | SC: 30% | ST: 25%
Part-B 60 Minutes 75 Questions / 75 Marks Qualifying only - trade-specific 35% for all candidates irrespective of category
Total Duration 175 Questions / 2.5 Hours  

Part-A Syllabus (CBT-2): Mathematics, General Intelligence and Reasoning, Basic Science and Engineering (Engineering Drawing, Units, Measurements, Basic Electricity, Levers, IT Literacy, etc.)

Part-B Syllabus (CBT-2) - Trade-wise Qualifying Test:

Sl. No. Qualification of Candidate Trade / Subject of Choice for Part-B
1 Electrical Engineering (and combinations) Electrician, Instrument Mechanic, Wireman, Armature & Coil Winder, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic
2 Electronics Engineering (and combinations) Electronics Mechanic, Mechanic (Radio & TV)
3 Mechanical Engineering (and combinations) Fitter, Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Turner, Machinist, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic, Heat Engine, Millwright/Maintenance Mechanic
4 Automobile Engineering (and combinations) Mechanic (Motor Vehicle), Tractor Mechanic, Mechanic (Diesel), Heat Engine, Refrigeration and Air-conditioning Mechanic

Stage 3 - Computer Based Aptitude Test (CBAT)

Shortlisting for CBAT 8 times the ALP vacancies per community/category per RRB (based on Part-A marks of CBT-2)
Qualifying Marks Minimum T-score of 42 in each test battery separately (all candidates irrespective of category)
Language English and Hindi only
Negative Marking No negative marking

Stage 4 & 5 - Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination (ME)

Final merit list is prepared RRB-wise from candidates qualifying in CBAT, with 50% weightage to Part-A of CBT-2 marks and 50% weightage to CBAT scores. Candidates equal to the number of vacancies are shortlisted for DV and Medical Examination. In case of a tie, the older candidate is preferred; if age is also equal, alphabetical order of given name (A to Z) is applied.

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Seniority of age (older candidate preferred)
2nd Tie-Break Alphabetical order (A to Z) of given name (not surname)

RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 - Medical Standards

The post of ALP carries Medical Standard A-1. Candidates must be physically fit in all respects with stringent vision requirements. Candidates who have undergone Lasik Surgery or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible. A Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Part-A signed by General Physician and Part-B signed by Eye Specialist, both from a Central/State Government Hospital or Medical College) must be submitted on the CBAT exam day.

Medical Standard General Fitness Vision Standards
A-1 Physically fit in all respects (i) Distant Vision: 6/6, 6/6 without glasses with fogging test (must not accept +2D); (ii) Near Vision: Sn: 0.6, 0.6 without glasses; (iii) Must pass tests for Colour Vision, Binocular Vision, Field of Vision, Night Vision, Mesopic Vision, etc.

How to Apply for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the official websites of participating RRBs (listed at Para 13.7 of the CEN). Hard copy / offline applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online portal is active from 15-05-2026 to 14-06-2026 (23:59 Hrs.). Candidates must first create an account on the RRB portal. Those who created an account for CENs notified in 2024/2025 should use the same credentials. Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and the chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation. Candidates can apply to only one RRB and submit only one application - multiple applications to the same or different RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and debarment from future RRB/RRC exams. Aadhaar or DigiLocker authentication is strongly advised during account creation for a smoother recruitment process. A live photograph is captured during the online application; pre-existing photographs are not accepted. No documents need to be uploaded at application stage (except signature and SC/ST certificate for free travel pass, if applicable).

Documents to be Produced at Document Verification (on being called):

  • Age proof - Matriculation / SSLC / 10th class certificate
  • Educational qualifications (Matriculation onwards) - marksheets and certificates
  • ITI / Diploma / Degree certificate in prescribed trade / discipline
  • SC / ST / OBC-NCL / EWS / Ex-Servicemen certificate (if applicable), in prescribed format and issued by competent authority
  • Self Screening Medical Declaration Report (Annexure VIII A & VIII B) - to be submitted on CBAT day
  • NOC from present employer (if applicable, for Government servants)

Candidates are advised to regularly visit the official RRB website for their chosen RRB for updates on all stages. Helpline for technical queries: Email: [email protected] | Phone: 0172-565-3333 and 9592001188 (10:00 AM to 5:00 PM on working days).

Apply Online at: Visit the official website of your chosen RRB (see list below)

Important Instructions for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only one application per candidate is accepted - multiple applications to one or more RRBs will result in rejection of all applications and permanent debarment from RRB/RRC exams.
  • Candidates awaiting results of their final prescribed qualification examination are NOT eligible to apply.
  • Candidates who have undergone Lasik or any other corrective eye surgery are not eligible for the post of ALP.
  • Selection of RRB, once submitted, is final and cannot be changed at any later date.
  • Details filled in the "Create an Account" form (including mobile number, email ID) and chosen RRB cannot be modified at any stage after account creation.
  • Examination fee is refundable (after deducting bank charges) only to candidates who appear in CBT-1; rejected or absent candidates are not eligible for refund.
  • Refund will be credited only to the Aadhaar-seeded bank account; candidates must ensure their Aadhaar is linked to an active bank account.
  • Mobile phones, electronic gadgets, watches, earphones, Bluetooth devices, calculators, books, pen, food items, and all other listed banned items are strictly prohibited inside the test centre.
  • Examination centre, date, and shift allotted in the e-call letter are final - no requests for change will be entertained under any circumstances.
  • Candidates must maintain identical signatures throughout all stages of the recruitment process. No BLOCK/CAPITAL/DISJOINTED letter signatures are acceptable.
  • Free Sleeper Class Railway Pass is available for SC/ST candidates for CBT/CBAT/DV/Medical Examination stages (if opted in application).
  • Candidates are advised to visit only official RRB websites and beware of fake websites, unscrupulous agents, and touts claiming to provide Railway jobs through influence or unfair means. RRBs never appoint agents or coaching centres for recruitment.

How to Calculate Your Score Using SSC Phase 13 Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score (based on standard SSC scheme):

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for SSC Selection Post Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +1 mark (or as per level)
  • Wrong Answer: -0.25 mark (negative marking)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks
